api/AudioVideoLib.AudioInfo.Analyse.html.summary: Method Analyse Namespace AudioVideoLib Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Analyse(Stream) Analyses the specified stream. public static AudioInfo Analyse(Stream stream) Parameters stream Stream The stream. Returns AudioInfo An AudioInfo instance containing information about the stream. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if stream is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.AudioInfo.AnalyseAsync.title: Method AnalyseAsync | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.AudioInfo.AnalyseAsync.summary: Method AnalyseAsync Namespace AudioVideoLib Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AnalyseAsync(Stream, CancellationToken) Asynchronously analyses the supplied stream. public static Task<AudioInfo> AnalyseAsync(Stream stream, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters stream Stream The stream to analyse. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. Returns Task<AudioInfo> A task that resolves to the populated AudioInfo. Remarks First-class async support is a follow-up: the underlying reader chain (ReadTags(Stream), ReadStreams(Stream)) is still synchronous. This overload runs the analysis on a background thread via Run(Action), which keeps the call site async-friendly without blocking the caller's thread but doesn't yet do true async I/O.

api/AudioVideoLib.AudioInfo.Save.html.title: Method Save | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.AudioInfo.Save.html.summary: Method Save Namespace AudioVideoLib Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Save(string, bool) Saves the AudioTags and MediaContainers to the specified file. public void Save(string file, bool overwrite = true) Parameters file string The destination file. overwrite bool When true (default), an existing file is overwritten; when false, an System.IO.IOException is thrown if the file exists. Exceptions IOException Thrown when the file exists and overwrite is false. Save(Stream) Writes the AudioTags and MediaContainers to the supplied stream. public void Save(Stream destination) Parameters destination Stream The destination stream. Remarks Tags with Start are written first (sorted by IAudioTagOffset.StartOffset), then each container, then tags with End. Container walkers use their streaming WriteTo implementation when available. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if destination is null.

Returns Task Remarks First-class async writes are a follow-up — see AnalyseAsync(Stream, CancellationToken) for the same caveat. Backed by Run(Action). SaveAsync(Stream, CancellationToken) Asynchronously writes the contents to the supplied stream. public Task SaveAsync(Stream destination, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters destination Stream The destination stream. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe for cancellation. api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.AsfObject.-cto.summary: Constructor AsfObject Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AsfObject(Guid, long, long) A single ASF (Advanced Systems Format) object discovered while walking a WMA / WMV / ASF container. public AsfObject(Guid Id, long StartOffset, long EndOffset) Parameters Id Guid The 16-byte object GUID, in its standard ASF mixed-endian binary layout. StartOffset long Offset of the object header (its GUID field) from the start of the stream. EndOffset long Offset immediately past the object payload. Remarks ASF object headers are laid out as a 16-byte GUID followed by a 64-bit little-endian object size that includes the header itself. The minimum legal size is therefore 24 bytes (16 + 8).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.AsfObject.html.summary: Class AsfObject Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A single ASF (Advanced Systems Format) object discovered while walking a WMA / WMV / ASF container. public sealed record AsfObject : IEquatable<AsfObject> Inheritance object AsfObject Implements IEquatable<AsfObject> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks ASF object headers are laid out as a 16-byte GUID followed by a 64-bit little-endian object size that includes the header itself. Fields HeaderSize The fixed size, in bytes, of an ASF object header (16-byte GUID + 8-byte size). Properties EndOffset Offset immediately past the object payload. Id The 16-byte object GUID, in its standard ASF mixed-endian binary layout. PayloadOffset Gets the offset of the object payload (the byte immediately after the header). PayloadSize Gets the size of the object payload in bytes (total size minus the 24-byte header). Size Gets the object's total length in bytes, including its 24-byte header. StartOffset Offset of the object header (its GUID field) from the start of the stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.BwfBextChunk.h.title: Class BwfBextChunk | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.BwfBextChunk.h.summary: Class BwfBextChunk Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A parsed Broadcast Wave Format bext chunk (EBU Tech 3285). public sealed class BwfBextChunk Inheritance object BwfBextChunk Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Layout (little-endian): Description[256], Originator[32], OriginatorReference[32], OriginationDate[10], OriginationTime[8], TimeReference (uint64), Version (uint16), UMID[64], (v1+) loudness fields (5 × int16), Reserved[180] (v1) / [180] (v2 same pad), CodingHistory (rest of chunk, ASCII). Fields MinV0Size The minimum size of a v0 bext chunk payload, in bytes. MinV1Size The minimum size of a v1+ bext chunk payload (adds 10 bytes of loudness fields, removes 10 from reserved). Properties CodingHistory Gets or sets the coding history (CRLF-separated ASCII, terminates the chunk). Description Gets or sets the description (max 256 ASCII chars). LoudnessRange Gets or sets the loudness range (×100, v1+). LoudnessValue Gets or sets the integrated loudness value (×100, v1+). MaxMomentaryLoudness Gets or sets the maximum momentary loudness (×100, v1+). MaxShortTermLoudness Gets or sets the maximum short-term loudness (×100, v1+). MaxTruePeakLevel Gets or sets the maximum true peak level (×100, v1+). OriginationDate Gets or sets the origination date (10 ASCII chars, "YYYY-MM-DD"). OriginationTime Gets or sets the origination time (8 ASCII chars, "HH:MM:SS"). Originator Gets or sets the originator (max 32 ASCII chars). OriginatorReference Gets or sets the originator reference (max 32 ASCII chars). TimeReference Gets or sets the time reference (samples since midnight) as a 64-bit value. Umid Gets or sets the SMPTE UMID (always 64 bytes; padded/truncated on write). Version Gets or sets the bext version (0, 1, or 2). Methods Parse(byte[]) Parses a bext chunk payload, or returns null for malformed input. Parse(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Parses a bext chunk payload, or returns null for malformed input. ToByteArray() Serialises the bext chunk into its on-disk payload bytes (excluding the 8-byte chunk header). ToChunkBytes() Serialises this bext chunk wrapped in a full bext RIFF chunk (8-byte header + payload + pad).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.title: Property IsCompactDisc | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.summary: Property IsCompactDisc Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsCompactDisc Gets a value indicating whether this instance corresponds to a compact disc. public bool IsCompactDisc { get; } Property Value bool true if this instance corresponds to a compact disc; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.title: Property LeadInSampleCount | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.summary: Property LeadInSampleCount Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll LeadInSampleCount Gets the number of lead-in samples. public long LeadInSampleCount { get; } Property Value long The number of lead-in samples. Remarks This field has meaning only for CD-DA cuesheets; for other uses it should be 0. For CD-DA, the lead-in is the TRACK 00 area where the table of contents is stored; more precisely, it is the number of samples from the first sample of the media to the first sample of the first index point of the first track. According to the Red Book, the lead-in must be silence and CD grabbing software does not usually store it; additionally, the lead-in must be at least two seconds but may be longer. For these reasons the lead-in length is stored here so that the absolute position of the first track can be computed. Note that the lead-in stored here is the number of samples up to the first index point of the first track, not necessarily to INDEX 01 of the first track; even the first track may have INDEX 00 data.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.title: Property MediaCatalogNumber | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetMe.summary: Property MediaCatalogNumber Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MediaCatalogNumber Gets the media catalog number. public string MediaCatalogNumber { get; } Property Value string The media catalog number. Remarks The media catalog number is in ASCII printable characters 0x20-0x7e. In general, the media catalog number may be 0 to 128 bytes long; any unused characters should be right-padded with NUL characters. For CD-DA, this is a thirteen digit number, followed by 115 NUL bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.title: Property TrackIndexCount | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property TrackIndexCount Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackIndexCount Gets the number of track index points. public int TrackIndexCount { get; } Property Value int The number of track index points. Remarks There must be at least one index in every track in a CUESHEET except for the lead-out track, which must have zero. For CD-DA, this number may be no more than 100.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.title: Property TrackIndexPoints | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property TrackIndexPoints Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackIndexPoints Gets the track index points. public IEnumerable<FlacCueSheetTrackIndexPoint> TrackIndexPoints { get; } Property Value IEnumerable<FlacCueSheetTrackIndexPoint> The track index points. Remarks For all tracks except the lead-out track, one or more track index points.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.title: Property TrackIsrc | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property TrackIsrc Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackIsrc Gets the International Standard Recording Code of the track. public string TrackIsrc { get; } Property Value string The International Standard Recording Code of the track. Remarks This is a 12-digit alphanumeric code. A value of 12 ASCII NUL characters may be used to denote absence of an ISRC.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.title: Property TrackNumber | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property TrackNumber Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackNumber Gets the track number. public int TrackNumber { get; } Property Value int The track number. Remarks A track number of 0 is not allowed to avoid conflicting with the CD-DA spec, which reserves this for the lead-in. For CD-DA the number must be 1-99, or 170 for the lead-out; for non-CD-DA, the track number must for 255 for the lead-out. It is not required but encouraged to start with track 1 and increase sequentially. Track numbers must be unique within a CUESHEET.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.title: Property TrackOffset | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property TrackOffset Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackOffset Gets the track offset in samples, relative to the beginning of the FLAC audio stream. public long TrackOffset { get; } Property Value long The track offset in samples, relative to the beginning of the FLAC audio stream. Remarks Track offset is the offset to the first index point of the track. (Note how this differs from CD-DA, where the track's offset in the TOC is that of the track's INDEX 01 even if there is an INDEX 00.) For CD-DA, the offset must be evenly divisible by 588 samples (588 samples = 44100 samples/sec * 1/75th of a sec).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.FlacCueSheetTr.summary: Property Offset Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Offset Gets the offset in samples, relative to the track offset, of the index point. public long Offset { get; } Property Value long The offset in samples, relative to the track offset, of the index point. Returns IxmlChunk Remarks Span-based overload: lets callers pass slices of an existing buffer without allocating an intermediate byte[]. The chunk still keeps its own raw-payload copy for byte-identical round-trip, so internally one allocation occurs.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.IxmlChunk.html.summary: Class IxmlChunk Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A parsed iXML chunk: UTF-8 XML metadata used in film/audio production (ixml.org). public sealed class IxmlChunk Inheritance object IxmlChunk Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The chunk's payload is captured verbatim. Common top-level fields under BWFXML are surfaced as properties; XML parse failures yield null property values rather than throws. Properties FileUid Gets the iXML FILE_UID field. IsWellFormed Gets a value indicating whether the XML payload parsed successfully. Note Gets the iXML NOTE field. ProjectName Gets the iXML PROJECT field, or null if absent or unparseable. RawPayload Gets the raw chunk payload bytes (BOM preserved if present). SceneName Gets the iXML SCENE field. TakeName Gets the iXML TAKE field. Tape Gets the iXML TAPE field. Ubits Gets the iXML UBITS field. Xml Gets the decoded UTF-8 XML string (BOM stripped). Methods Parse(byte[]) Parses an iXML chunk payload. Returns null for empty input; otherwise always returns a chunk (well-formedness is reported via IsWellFormed). Parse(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Parses an iXML chunk payload. Returns null for empty input; otherwise always returns a chunk (well-formedness is reported via IsWellFormed). ToByteArray() Returns the chunk's raw payload bytes (the exact bytes seen at parse time). ToByteArray(bool) Returns the XML payload, optionally with a leading UTF-8 BOM, as a fresh byte array. ToChunkBytes() Serialises this iXML chunk wrapped in a full iXML RIFF chunk (8-byte header + payload + pad).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.-ctor..title: Constructor LameTag | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.-ctor..summary: Constructor LameTag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll LameTag(StreamBuffer) Initializes a new instance of the LameTag class. public LameTag(StreamBuffer firstFrameBuffer) Parameters firstFrameBuffer StreamBuffer The first frame buffer containing a LameTag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.AthTyp.title: Property AthType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.AthTyp.summary: Property AthType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AthType Gets the LAME ATH Type. public int AthType { get; } Property Value int Remarks 4 LSB: LAME ATH Type examples: byte 0xAF = 03h = 0000 0011b => 4 MSB = 0000b = 0d : LAME does NOT use --nspsytune, LAME does NOT use --nssafejoint 4 LSB = 0011b = 3d : ATH type 3 used byte 0xAF = 15h = 0001 0101b => 4 MSB = 0001b = 3d : LAME does use --nspsytune, LAME does NOT use --nssafejoint 4 LSB = 0101b = 5d : ATH type 5 used

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Audiop.title: Property AudiophileReplayGain | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Audiop.summary: Property AudiophileReplayGain Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AudiophileReplayGain Gets the audiophile replay gain. public short AudiophileReplayGain { get; } Property Value short Remarks 16 bit "Audiophile Replay Gain" field, required to give ideal listening loudness from http://privatewww.essex.ac.uk/~djmrob/replaygain/rg_data_format.html bits 0h-2h: NAME of Gain adjustment: 000 = not set 001 = radio 010 = audiophile (see - room for plenty more!) bits 3h-5h: ORIGINATOR of Gain adjustment: 000 = not set 001 = set by artist 010 = set by user 011 = set by my model 100 = set by simple RMS average etc etc (see - room for plenty more again!) bit 6h: Sign bit bits 7h-Fh: ABSOLUTE GAIN ADJUSTMENT storing 10x the adjustment (to give the extra decimal place).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.BitRat.title: Property BitRate | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.BitRat.summary: Property BitRate Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll BitRate Gets the bitrate. public int BitRate { get; } Property Value int Remarks if ABR {specified bitrate} else {minimal bitrate} IF the file is an ABR file: range: 01h = 01d : 1 kbit/s (--abr 1) -> FFh = 255d : 255 kbit/s or larger (--abr 255) value 00h => unknown examples: byte 0xB0 = C3h C3h = 195d : --abr 195 byte 0xB0 = 78h 78h = 128d : --abr 128 byte 0xB0 = FEh FEh = 254d : --abr 254 byte 0xB0 = FFh FEh = 255d : --abr 255 or higher, eg: --abr 280 IF the file is NOT an ABR file: (CBR/VBR) the (CBR)/(minimal VBR (-b)) bitrate is stored here 8-255. 255 if bigger. examples: "LAME -V0 -b224" will store (224)d=(E0)h "LAME -b320" will store (255)d=(FF)h

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.title: Property EncoderDelayPaddingSamples | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.summary: Property EncoderDelayPaddingSamples Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncoderDelayPaddingSamples Gets the encoder delay padding samples. public int EncoderDelayPaddingSamples { get; } Property Value int

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.title: Property EncoderDelaySamples | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.summary: Property EncoderDelaySamples Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncoderDelaySamples Gets the encoder delay samples. public int EncoderDelaySamples { get; } Property Value int

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.title: Property EncoderDelays | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.summary: Property EncoderDelays Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncoderDelays Gets the encoder delays. public int EncoderDelays { get; } Property Value int Remarks Encoder delays store in 3 bytes: [xxxxxxxx][xxxxyyyy][yyyyyyyy] the 12 bit values (0-4095) of how many samples were added at start (encoder delay) in X and how many 0-samples were padded at the end in Y to complete the last frame. so ideally you could do: #frames * (#samples / frame) - (these two values) = exact number of samples in original wav. so worst case scenario you'd have a 48kHz file which would give it a range of 0.085s at the end and at the start. example: [01101100][00010010][11010010] X = (011011000001)b = (1729)d, so 1729 samples is the encoder delay Y = (001011010010)b = (722)d, so 722 samples have been padded at the end of the file

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.title: Property EncoderVersion | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encode.summary: Property EncoderVersion Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncoderVersion Gets the encoder version. public string EncoderVersion { get; } Property Value string

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encodi.title: Property EncodingFlags | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Encodi.summary: Property EncodingFlags Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncodingFlags Gets the encoding flags. public int EncodingFlags { get; } Property Value int Remarks 4 MSB: 4 encoding flags 000? LAME uses "--nspsytune", ? = 0 : false ? = 1 : true 00?0 LAME uses "--nssafejoint" ? = 0 : false ? = 1 : true 0?00 This track is --nogap continued in a next track ? = 0 : false ? = 1 : true is true for all but the last track in a --nogap album ?000 This track is the --nogap continuation of an earlier one ? = 0 : false ? = 1 : true is true for all but the first track in a --nogap album

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Lowpas.summary: Property LowpassFilterValue Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll LowpassFilterValue Gets the low pass filter value, in Hz. public int LowpassFilterValue { get; } Property Value int Remarks low pass = (low pass value) / 100 range: 01h = 01d : 100Hz -> FFh = 255d : 25500Hz value 00h => unknown examples: byte 0xA6 = C3h C3h = 195d : 19500Hz byte 0xA6 = 78h 78h = 120d : 12000Hz

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Misc.h.title: Property Misc | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Misc.h.summary: Property Misc Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Misc Gets the misc. public int Misc { get; } Property Value int Remarks 2 lsb I'd like to add the different noise shapings also in a 2-bit field (0-3) (00)b: noise shaping: 0 (01)b: noise shaping: 1 (10)b: noise shaping: 2 (11)b: noise shaping: 3 3 bits Stereo mode msb fist: (000)b: (m)ono (001)b: (s)tereo (010)b: (d)ual (011)b: (j)oint (100)b: (f)orce (101)b: (a)uto (110)b: (i)ntensity (111)b: (x)undefined / different 1 bit unwise settings used (0)b: no (1)b: yes (definition encoder side()) ()some settings were used which would likely damage quality in normal circumstances. (like disabling all use of the ATH or forcing only short blocks, -b192 ...) 2 msb Source (not mp3) sample frequency (00)b: 32kHz or smaller (01)b: 44.1kHz (10)b: 48kHz (11)b: higher than 48kHz

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Mp3Gai.title: Property Mp3Gain | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Mp3Gai.summary: Property Mp3Gain Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Mp3Gain Gets the MP3 gain. public int Mp3Gain { get; } Property Value int Remarks any mp3 can be amplified by a factor 2 ^ ( x * 0.25) in a lossless manner by a tool like eg. mp3gain byte 0xB5 is set to (00)h by default. if done so, this 8-bit field can be used to log such transformation happened so that any given time it can be undone.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.MusicC.title: Property MusicCrc | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.MusicC.summary: Property MusicCrc Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MusicCrc Gets the CRC-16 of the complete mp3 music data as originally produced by LAME. public short MusicCrc { get; } Property Value short Remarks If an application like mp3gain changes the main music frames of the mp3 (for instance via the Mp3Gain field), this CRC becomes invalid. The InfoTagCrc can still be valid however - tools can update it.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.MusicL.title: Property MusicLength | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.MusicL.summary: Property MusicLength Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MusicLength Gets the length of the music, in bytes. This is not the length of audio in MS, but rather the size of the audio in bytes. public int MusicLength { get; } Property Value int The length of the music. Remarks 32 bit integer filed containing the exact length in bytes of the mp3 file originally made by LAME excluded ID3 tag info at the end. The first byte it counts is the first byte of this LAME Tag and the last byte it counts is the last byte of the last mp3 frame containing music. Should be file length at the time of LAME encoding, except when using ID3 tags. practical example: {misc+Id3v2 tag info}[LAME Tag frame][complete mp3 music data]{misc+Id3v1/2 tag info} where contents between {} are not included in the Music Length. remark: applying any (Id3v2) kind of tagging or information in FRONT of the LAME/Xing Tag frame is a very bad idea. You will disable the functionality of all decoders to read the tag info correctly. (for example: VBR mp3 seek info will no longer be usable) range (1)d-(4,294,967,295)d [ or about 4294967295/(65010241024)/320*1411 = 27.79 hours of 44.1kHz 320kbit/s music. ] Music length not set / unknown / larger than 4G: 0xB8 0xB9 0xBA 0xBB 00h 00h 00h 00h use of this field: together with the next field deliver an effective verification of music's integrity and effective shield from TAGs.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.PeakSi.title: Property PeakSignalAmplitude | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.PeakSi.summary: Property PeakSignalAmplitude Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PeakSignalAmplitude Gets the peak signal amplitude. public float PeakSignalAmplitude { get; } Property Value float Remarks 32 bit floating point "Peak signal amplitude" 1.0 is maximal signal amplitude storable in decoding format. 0.8 is 80% of maximal signal amplitude storable in decoding format. 1.5 is 150% of maximal signal amplitude storable in decoding format.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Preset.title: Property PresetSurroundInfo | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.Preset.summary: Property PresetSurroundInfo Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PresetSurroundInfo Gets the preset surround info. public short PresetSurroundInfo { get; } Property Value short Remarks 2 most significant bits: unused 3 bits: surround info 0: no surround info 1: DPL encoding 2: DPL2 encoding 3: Ambisonic encoding 8: reserved 11 least significant bits: Preset used. 0: unknown/ no preset used This allows a range of 2047 presets. With Lame we would use the value of the internal preset enum.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.RadioR.title: Property RadioReplayGain | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.RadioR.summary: Property RadioReplayGain Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RadioReplayGain Gets the radio replay gain. public short RadioReplayGain { get; } Property Value short Remarks 16 bit "Radio Replay Gain" field, required to make all tracks equal loudness. from http://privatewww.essex.ac.uk/~djmrob/replaygain/rg_data_format.html bits 0h-2h: NAME of Gain adjustment: 000 = not set 001 = radio 010 = audiophile (see - room for plenty more!) bits 3h-5h: ORIGINATOR of Gain adjustment: 000 = not set 001 = set by artist 010 = set by user 011 = set by my model 100 = set by simple RMS average etc etc (see - room for plenty more again!) bit 6h: Sign bit bits 7h-Fh: ABSOLUTE GAIN ADJUSTMENT storing 10x the adjustment (to give the extra decimal place).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.html.title: Class LameTag | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.LameTag.html.summary: Class LameTag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The LAME tag used to store more VBR information, usually it's located at the XING header. public sealed class LameTag Inheritance object LameTag Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The purpose of this tag is to provide extra information about the mp3 bit stream, encoder and parameters used. This tag should, as much as possible, be meaningful for as many encoders as possible, even if it is unlikely that other encoders than Lame will implement it. Constructors LameTag(StreamBuffer) Initializes a new instance of the LameTag class. Properties AthType Gets the LAME ATH Type. AudiophileReplayGain Gets the audiophile replay gain. BitRate Gets the bitrate. EncoderDelayPaddingSamples Gets the encoder delay padding samples. EncoderDelaySamples Gets the encoder delay samples. EncoderDelays Gets the encoder delays. EncoderVersion Gets the encoder version. EncodingFlags Gets the encoding flags. InfoTagCrc Gets the CRC-16 of the first 190 bytes (0x00 - 0xBD) of the Info header frame. InfoTagRevision Gets the info tag revision. IsAbr Gets a value indicating whether this instance is ABR. IsCbr Gets a value indicating whether this instance is CBR. IsVbr Gets a value indicating whether this instance is VBR. LowpassFilterValue Gets the low pass filter value, in Hz. Misc Gets the misc. Mp3Gain Gets the MP3 gain. MusicCrc Gets the CRC-16 of the complete mp3 music data as originally produced by LAME. MusicLength Gets the length of the music, in bytes. This is not the length of audio in MS, but rather the size of the audio in bytes. PeakSignalAmplitude Gets the peak signal amplitude. PresetSurroundInfo Gets the preset surround info. RadioReplayGain Gets the radio replay gain. VbrMethod Gets the VBR method. VbrMethodName Gets the name of the VBR method. Version Gets the LAME version. Methods FindTag(StreamBuffer, long) Finds a LameTag in the given stream. ToByteArray() Places the LameTag into a byte array.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4Box.-ctor.h.title: Constructor Mp4Box | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4Box.-ctor.h.summary: Constructor Mp4Box Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Mp4Box(string, long, long, int) A single ISO/IEC 14496-12 box (atom) discovered while walking an MP4 / M4A container. public Mp4Box(string Type, long StartOffset, long EndOffset, int HeaderSize) Parameters Type string The four-character atom type (e.g. "moov", "udta", "©nam", "covr"). Surrogate pair © in iTunes atoms is encoded as the single byte 0xA9; this property keeps the original 4-character string after Latin-1 decoding so callers can match by literal. StartOffset long Offset of the atom header (its size field) from the start of the stream. EndOffset long Offset immediately past the atom payload. HeaderSize int Size of the atom header in bytes (8 for 32-bit size, 16 for 64-bit extended size).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4Box.html.summary: Class Mp4Box Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A single ISO/IEC 14496-12 box (atom) discovered while walking an MP4 / M4A container. public sealed record Mp4Box : IEquatable<Mp4Box> Inheritance object Mp4Box Implements IEquatable<Mp4Box> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Mp4Box(string, long, long, int) A single ISO/IEC 14496-12 box (atom) discovered while walking an MP4 / M4A container. Properties EndOffset Offset immediately past the atom payload. HeaderSize Size of the atom header in bytes (8 for 32-bit size, 16 for 64-bit extended size). api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4DataType.ht.title: Enum Mp4DataType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4DataType.ht.summary: Enum Mp4DataType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Identifies how the payload of an ilst item's data sub-atom is encoded. public enum Mp4DataType Fields BeSignedInt = 21 Big-endian signed integer (1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes). BeUnsignedInt = 22 Big-endian unsigned integer (1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes). Implicit = 0 Reserved / implicit type — payload format is implied by the parent atom name. Jpeg = 13 JPEG image. Png = 14 PNG image. Utf16 = 2 UTF-16 text without null terminator. Utf8 = 1 UTF-8 text without null terminator. Remarks Values follow the type codes defined by the QuickTime / iTunes metadata format. Only the values needed for audio metadata are listed; unknown codes are reported verbatim.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4MetaItem.ht.title: Class Mp4MetaItem | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.Mp4MetaItem.ht.summary: Class Mp4MetaItem Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A single child of an ilst atom (one iTunes metadata field). public sealed class Mp4MetaItem Inheritance object Mp4MetaItem Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Each ilst child wraps a single data sub-atom that carries: 4-byte type code, 4-byte locale (skipped), then the payload. Free-form ---- items also carry mean and name sub-atoms that identify the namespace and key. Constructors Mp4MetaItem(string, Mp4DataType, byte[], string?, string?) Initializes a new instance of the Mp4MetaItem class. Properties AtomType Gets the four-character atom type. DataType Gets the data sub-atom's type code. Mean Gets the mean string for free-form items (e.g. "com.apple.iTunes"); null for standard items. Name Gets the name string for free-form items (e.g. "MusicBrainz Track Id"); null for standard items. Payload Gets the raw payload bytes. Methods AsIndexTotalPair() Decodes a trkn / disk-style payload: 8 bytes of reserved/index/total/reserved as four big-endian int16 values. AsInteger() Decodes the payload as a big-endian integer of width 1, 2, 4, or 8 bytes. AsUtf8String() Gets the payload decoded as a UTF-8 string, regardless of the declared type. BuildIndexTotalPayload(int, int) Builds a payload encoding the trkn / disk layout: 4 × big-endian int16 (reserved/index/total/reserved).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Crc.h.title: Property Crc | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Crc.h.summary: Property Crc Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Crc Gets the CRC value of the frame. public int Crc { get; } Property Value int The CRC value of the frame. Remarks The CRC field will be set with the CRC when IsCrcProtected is true.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Empha.title: Property Emphasis | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Empha.summary: Property Emphasis Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Emphasis Gets the Emphasis used for this MPEG frame. public MpaFrameEmphasis Emphasis { get; } Property Value MpaFrameEmphasis The Emphasis for this frame, if used; otherwise 0. Possible values are Half (50/15 milliseconds) or CCIT J.17.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.EndOf.title: Property EndOffset | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.EndOf.summary: Property EndOffset Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IAudioFrame, where it ends in the stream. public long EndOffset { get; } Property Value long The end offset of the IAudioFrame, counting from the start of the stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.title: Field FrameHeaderSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.summary: Field FrameHeaderSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A MPEGFrame header is always 4 bytes. public const int FrameHeaderSize = 4 Returns int A MPEGFrame header is always 4 bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.title: Property FrameLength | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.summary: Property FrameLength Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FrameLength Gets the frame length, in bytes. public int FrameLength { get; } Property Value int The length of the frame, in bytes. Remarks Frame length is the length of a frame in bytes when compressed. This includes the size of the header and the size of the audio data.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.title: Property FrameSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.summary: Property FrameSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FrameSize Gets the frame size of the current frame, this is the number of samples contained in the frame. public int FrameSize { get; } Property Value int The number of samples in the frame. Remarks Frame size is the number of samples contained in a frame. It is constant and always 384 samples for Layer I, 1152 samples for Layer II and 576 (MPEG 2 and 2.5) or 1152 (MPEG 1) samples for Layer III. This is NOT the size of the frame itself. See FrameLength to retrieve the size of the full frame.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.title: Property FrameSizeMultiplier | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Frame.summary: Property FrameSizeMultiplier Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FrameSizeMultiplier Gets the frame multiplier size of the current frame. public int FrameSizeMultiplier { get; } Property Value int The multiplier for the current frame based on the AudioVersion and the LayerVersion.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsCop.title: Property IsCopyrighted | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsCop.summary: Property IsCopyrighted Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsCopyrighted Gets a value indicating whether this frame is has the copyright flag set. public bool IsCopyrighted { get; } Property Value bool true if this frame is copyrighted; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsCrc.title: Property IsCrcProtected | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsCrc.summary: Property IsCrcProtected Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsCrcProtected Gets a value indicating whether this MPEG frame is protected by a CRC that's appended after the header. public bool IsCrcProtected { get; } Property Value bool true if this frame is CRC protected; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsMon.title: Property IsMono | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsMon.summary: Property IsMono Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsMono Gets a value indicating whether this frame is in mono. public bool IsMono { get; } Property Value bool true if this frame is in mono; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsOri.title: Property IsOriginalMedia | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsOri.summary: Property IsOriginalMedia Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsOriginalMedia Gets a value indicating whether the MPEG frame is from the original media. public bool IsOriginalMedia { get; } Property Value bool true if this frame is from the original media; otherwise, false if it's from a copy.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsPad.title: Property IsPadded | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsPad.summary: Property IsPadded Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsPadded Gets a value indicating whether the MPEG frame is padded with an extra slot or not. public bool IsPadded { get; } Property Value bool true if this frame is padded; otherwise, false. Remarks See SlotSize to retrieve the slot size, in bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsPri.title: Property IsPrivateBitSet | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsPri.summary: Property IsPrivateBitSet Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsPrivateBitSet Gets a value indicating whether the private bit is set for this frame. public bool IsPrivateBitSet { get; } Property Value bool true if this instance is private; otherwise, false. Remarks This bit is only informative; it may be freely used for specific needs of an application, i.e. if it has to trigger some application specific events.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsVal.title: Property IsValidBitrate | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsVal.summary: Property IsValidBitrate Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsValidBitrate Gets a value indicating whether the bitrate is valid. public bool IsValidBitrate { get; } Property Value bool true if this instance is valid bitrate; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsVal.title: Property IsValidSampingRate | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.IsVal.summary: Property IsValidSampingRate Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsValidSampingRate Gets a value indicating whether the valid sampling rate is valid. public bool IsValidSampingRate { get; } Property Value bool true if this instance is valid sampling rate; otherwise, false.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Layer.title: Property LayerVersion | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Layer.summary: Property LayerVersion Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll LayerVersion Gets the layer version used in this frame. public MpaFrameLayerVersion LayerVersion { get; } Property Value MpaFrameLayerVersion The Layer Version, this can be one of the following: Layer I, Layer II or Layer III.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.ModeE.title: Property ModeExtension | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.ModeE.summary: Property ModeExtension Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ModeExtension Gets the Mode extension used for this MPEG frame. public int ModeExtension { get; } Property Value int The mode extension used for this MPEG frame, this is different depending on the LayerVersionused, see remarks. Remarks Mode extension is used to join information that are of no use for stereo effect, thus reducing needed resources. These bits are dynamically determined by an encoder in Joint stereo mode.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.ReadF.title: Method ReadFrame | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.ReadF.summary: Method ReadFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ReadFrame(Stream) Reads a MpaFrame from a System.IO.Stream. public static MpaFrame? api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Sampl.title: Property SampleSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Sampl.summary: Property SampleSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SampleSize Gets the size of a sample, in bits. public int SampleSize { get; } Property Value int The size of a sample, in bits.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Sampl.title: Property SamplingRate | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Sampl.summary: Property SamplingRate Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SamplingRate Gets the sampling rate per second in Hz of the audio. public int SamplingRate { get; } Property Value int The sampling rate per second, in Hz, of the audio.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.SideI.title: Property SideInfoSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.SideI.summary: Property SideInfoSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SideInfoSize Gets the size of the side info. public int SideInfoSize { get; } Property Value int The size of the side info, in bytes. Remarks The side information follows the header or the CRC in Layer III files. It contains information about the general decoding of the frame, but doesn't contain the actual encoded audio samples.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.SlotS.title: Property SlotSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.SlotS.summary: Property SlotSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SlotSize Gets the size of the slot of the current frame. public int SlotSize { get; } Property Value int The slot size for this MPEG frame. Remarks For Layer I a slot is 32 bits (4 bytes) long, for Layer II and Layer III a slot is 8 bit (1 byte) long.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.Start.summary: Property StartOffset Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IAudioFrame, where it starts in the stream. public long StartOffset { get; } Property Value long The start offset of the IAudioFrame, counting from the start of the stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.ToByt.summary: Method ToByteArray Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ToByteArray() Returns the frame in a byte array. public byte[] ToByteArray() Returns byte[] The frame in a byte array.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.html.title: Class MpaFrame | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrame.html.summary: Class MpaFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Public class for MPEG audio frames. An MPEG audio file consists out of frames. Each frame contains of a header followed by the audio data. public sealed class MpaFrame : IAudioFrame Inheritance object MpaFrame Implements IAudioFrame Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields FrameHeaderSize A MPEGFrame header is always 4 bytes. Properties AudioData Gets the audio data. AudioDataLength Gets the length of the audio data, in bytes. AudioLength Gets the length of audio, in milliseconds. AudioVersion Gets the audio version used in this frame. Bitrate Gets the bitrate of the audio, in KBit. BitrateInBitsPerSecond Gets the bitrate in bits per second of the audio. BitrateInBytesPerSecond Gets the bitrate in bytes per second of the audio. ChannelMode Gets the channel mode this frame is encoded in. Crc Gets the CRC value of the frame. Emphasis Gets the Emphasis used for this MPEG frame. EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IAudioFrame, where it ends in the stream. FrameLength Gets the frame length, in bytes. FrameSize Gets the frame size of the current frame, this is the number of samples contained in the frame. FrameSizeMultiplier Gets the frame multiplier size of the current frame. IsCopyrighted Gets a value indicating whether this frame is has the copyright flag set. IsCrcProtected Gets a value indicating whether this MPEG frame is protected by a CRC that's appended after the header. IsMono Gets a value indicating whether this frame is in mono. IsOriginalMedia Gets a value indicating whether the MPEG frame is from the original media. IsPadded Gets a value indicating whether the MPEG frame is padded with an extra slot or not. IsPrivateBitSet Gets a value indicating whether the private bit is set for this frame. IsValidBitrate Gets a value indicating whether the bitrate is valid. IsValidSampingRate Gets a value indicating whether the valid sampling rate is valid. LayerVersion Gets the layer version used in this frame. ModeExtension Gets the Mode extension used for this MPEG frame. SampleSize Gets the size of a sample, in bits. SamplingRate Gets the sampling rate per second in Hz of the audio. SideInfoSize Gets the size of the side info. SlotSize Gets the size of the slot of the current frame. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IAudioFrame, where it starts in the stream. Methods CalculateCrc() Calculates the CRC of the MpaFrame. ReadFrame(Stream) Reads a MpaFrame from a System.IO.Stream. ToByteArray() Returns the frame in a byte array.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrameEmphas.title: Enum MpaFrameEmphasis | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrameEmphas.summary: Enum MpaFrameEmphasis Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Enumerator for the Emphasis bit. public enum MpaFrameEmphasis Fields Ccit = 3 Emphasis is set to CCIT J.17. Half = 1 Emphasis is set to 50/15 milliseconds. None = 0 No emphasis set. Reserved = 2 Reserved field.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrameLayerV.title: Enum MpaFrameLayerVersion | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.MpaFrameLayerV.summary: Enum MpaFrameLayerVersion Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Enumerator for the Layer version bit. public enum MpaFrameLayerVersion Fields Layer1 = 3 Layer is Layer I. Layer2 = 2 Layer is Layer II. Layer3 = 1 Layer is Layer III. Reserved = 0 Reserved field.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.OggPage.-ctor..title: Constructor OggPage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.OggPage.-ctor..summary: Constructor OggPage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OggPage(long, long, int, byte, long, int, int, int, int, int) A single page within an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533). public OggPage(long StartOffset, long EndOffset, int Version, byte HeaderFlags, long GranulePosition, int SerialNumber, int SequenceNumber, int Checksum, int SegmentCount, int PayloadSize) Parameters StartOffset long Offset of the page header (the "OggS" magic) from the start of the stream. EndOffset long Offset immediately past the page payload. Version int Ogg page structure version. Always 0 for the current spec. HeaderFlags byte Page header type flags (continuation / BOS / EOS bits). GranulePosition long Codec-specific position marker — typically a sample count for Vorbis/Opus. SerialNumber int Logical bitstream identifier; pages sharing a serial number belong to the same stream. SequenceNumber int Monotonically increasing page counter within the logical bitstream. Checksum int CRC-32 checksum from the page header (not recomputed by the walker). SegmentCount int Number of entries in the page's segment table. PayloadSize int Total size of the page payload, in bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.OggPage.html.summary: Class OggPage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A single page within an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533). public sealed record OggPage : IEquatable<OggPage> Inheritance object OggPage Implements IEquatable<OggPage> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors OggPage(long, long, int, byte, long, int, int, int, int, int) A single page within an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533). PayloadSize Total size of the page payload, in bytes. SegmentCount Number of entries in the page's segment table. SequenceNumber Monotonically increasing page counter within the logical bitstream. SerialNumber Logical bitstream identifier; pages sharing a serial number belong to the same stream. Size Gets the page's total length in bytes, including its header and segment table. api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.RiffInfoTag.ht.summary: Class RiffInfoTag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll A parsed WAV LIST chunk with form-type INFO: the RIFF text-metadata block. public sealed class RiffInfoTag Inheritance object RiffInfoTag Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The standard four-character codes (INAM, IART, IPRD, ICRD, ICMT, IGNR, ITRK, IENG, ISFT, ICOP) are surfaced as named properties. All other sub-chunks fall through to Items. Properties Artist Gets the artist (IART). Comment Gets the comment (ICMT). Copyright Gets the copyright (ICOP). CreationDate Gets the creation date (ICRD). Engineer Gets the engineer (IENG). Genre Gets the genre (IGNR). Items Gets the full set of parsed key/value items, keyed by four-character chunk id. Product Gets the product / album (IPRD). Software Gets the software (ISFT). Title Gets the title (INAM). Track Gets the track number (ITRK). Methods FromListPayload(byte[]) Parses a full WAV LIST chunk payload — including the leading 4-byte form-type — into a tag, or returns null if the form-type is not INFO or input is malformed. FromListPayload(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Parses a full WAV LIST chunk payload — including the leading 4-byte form-type — into a tag, or returns null if the form-type is not INFO or input is malformed. Parse(byte[]) Parses a LIST chunk payload (form-type INFO already consumed) into a tag, or returns null for malformed input. Parse(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) Parses a LIST chunk payload (form-type INFO already consumed) into a tag, or returns null for malformed input. SetItem(string, string?) Sets or replaces a single item by its four-character id. Use null to remove. ToByteArray() Serialises the tag's sub-chunks (without the enclosing LIST header or form-type). Sub-chunk values are written as null-terminated ASCII and word-aligned. ToListChunkBytes() Serialises the tag wrapped in a full LIST INFO chunk (8-byte header + form-type + items).

api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.html.title: Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Formats.html.summary: Namespace AudioVideoLib.Formats Classes AiffChunk A single chunk inside an AIFF or AIFF-C container. AiffComment A single comment record from an AIFF COMT chunk. AiffTextChunks Bundle of the standard text-bearing chunks defined by the AIFF (1989) specification: NAME, AUTH, ANNO, and COMT. AsfObject A single ASF (Advanced Systems Format) object discovered while walking a WMA / WMV / ASF container. BwfBextChunk A parsed Broadcast Wave Format bext chunk (EBU Tech 3285). DsdChunk A single chunk inside a DSD audio container — either Sony DSF (.dsf) or Philips DFF (.dff). EbmlElement A single element discovered while walking an EBML (Matroska / WebM) container. FlacApplicationMetadataBlock Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacConstantSubFrame Represents a constant-signal subframe. FlacCueSheetMetadataBlock FlacCueSheetTrack Class to contain a FLAC cue sheet track. FlacCueSheetTrackIndexPoint Track index point. FlacFixedSubFrame Represents a fixed-predictor subframe. FlacFrame Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacLinearPredictorSubFrame Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacMetadataBlock Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacPaddingMetadataBlock Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacPictureMetadataBlock Class for storing an attached picture frame. FlacResidual FlacRicePartition Represents a Rice-coded residual partition. FlacSeekPoint FlacSeekTableMetadataBlock FlacStreamInfoMetadataBlock Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacSubFrame Class for FLAC audio sub frames. FlacVerbatimSubFrame Class for FLAC audio frames. FlacVorbisCommentsMetadataBlock Class for FLAC audio frames. IxmlChunk A parsed iXML chunk: UTF-8 XML metadata used in film/audio production (ixml.org). LameTag The LAME tag used to store more VBR information, usually it's located at the XING header. Mp4Box A single ISO/IEC 14496-12 box (atom) discovered while walking an MP4 / M4A container. Mp4MetaItem A single child of an ilst atom (one iTunes metadata field). MpaFrame Public class for MPEG audio frames. An MPEG audio file consists out of frames. Each frame contains of a header followed by the audio data. OggPage A single page within an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533). RiffChunk A single chunk inside a RIFF container (e.g. WAV). RiffInfoTag A parsed WAV LIST chunk with form-type INFO: the RIFF text-metadata block. VbrHeader VBRHeader base class for VBR headers. VBR classes MUST derive from this class. VbriHeader VBRI VBR Header used to store VBR information. XingHeader XING VBR Header used to store VBR information. Structs InfoTagRevision Possible Info Tag revision values. XingHeaderFlags XING VBR Header flags. Flags which indicate what fields are present, flags are combined with a logical OR. Field is mandatory. Interfaces IAudioFormat Interface for an audio format to implement. IAudioFrame Interface for audio frames. Enums FlacBlockingStrategy FlacChannelAssignment Flac channel assignment. FlacCueSheetPreEmphasis The pre-emphasis flag. This corresponds to the CD-DA Q-channel control bit 5. FlacCueSheetTrackType The track type. This corresponds to the CD-DA Q-channel control bit 3. FlacMetadataBlockType Enum for the metadata block types. FlacPictureType Picture types. FlacResidualCodingMethod The residual coding methods. FlacSubFrameType Picture types. Mp4DataType Identifies how the payload of an ilst item's data sub-atom is encoded. MpaAudioVersion Enumerator for the MPEG Audio Version bit. MpaChannelMode Enumerator for the Channel Mode bit. MpaFrameEmphasis Enumerator for the Emphasis bit. MpaFrameLayerVersion Enumerator for the Layer version bit. VbrHeaderType Possible VBR header types in a MPA stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AiffStream.html.title: Class AiffStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AiffStream.html.summary: Class AiffStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for AIFF and AIFF-C containers. Does not decode audio samples. public sealed class AiffStream : IMediaContainer Inheritance object AiffStream Implements IMediaContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker captures the COMM chunk needed to report format parameters and locates the SSND chunk, while recording every other chunk structurally without materialising its payload. Sample rate is decoded from the 80-bit IEEE 754 extended-precision value stored by the AIFF spec. Properties Channels Gets the channel count from the COMM chunk. Chunks Gets the chunks discovered inside the AIFF container, in file order. Compression Gets the four-character compression identifier from an AIFF-C COMM chunk, or null for plain AIFF. MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. SampleFrames Gets the total number of sample frames from the COMM chunk. SampleRate Gets the sample rate in Hz, decoded from the 80-bit IEEE 754 extended-precision value in the COMM chunk. SampleSize Gets the number of bits per sample from the COMM chunk. SsndOffset Gets the absolute offset of the SSND chunk's payload, or 0 if the chunk is absent. SsndSize Gets the size of the SSND chunk's payload in bytes. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. TextChunks Gets the bundle of standard AIFF text chunks (NAME, AUTH, ANNO, COMT) discovered while walking the container, or null if none were present. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AsfStream.WriteTo.h.title: Method WriteTo | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AsfStream.WriteTo.h.summary: Method WriteTo Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination. public void WriteTo(Stream destination) Parameters destination Stream The stream to write the container bytes into. Remarks Streams the rebuilt Header Object directly to the destination, then streams the unchanged Data + Index regions from the source via ISourceReader. Peak memory is bounded by the Header Object size, not the file size. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If destination is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AsfStream.html.title: Class AsfStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.AsfStream.html.summary: Class AsfStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for ASF (Advanced Systems Format) containers — i.e. .asf, .wma, and .wmv files. Does not decode media payload. public sealed class AsfStream : IMediaContainer, IDisposable Inheritance object AsfStream Implements IMediaContainer IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker validates the top-level Header Object GUID and enumerates its child objects, recording each one as an AsfObject. Recognised metadata-bearing children (Content Description, Extended Content Description, Metadata, Metadata Library — the last two nested inside the Header Extension Object) are parsed into MetadataTag. Unrecognised objects are recorded generically and skipped without failing the walk. Only the (small) ASF Header Object is materialised in memory; the much larger Data Object and any Index Object that follow are kept reachable via an ISourceReader reference and streamed straight to the destination at write time. Editing metadata in a multi-GB WMV is bounded by the header size, not the file size. Callers must keep the source System.IO.Stream passed to ReadStream(Stream) alive until WriteTo(Stream) / ToByteArray finishes. Properties EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IMediaContainer, where it ends in the stream. HeaderObjectChildrenOffset Gets the absolute offset of the Header Object payload (the byte immediately after its 30-byte header + 6-byte child-count + reserved fields). 0 if the file was not parsed. HeaderObjectSize Gets the size of the Header Object as recorded in its on-disk size field, or 0. MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. MetadataTag Gets the parsed ASF metadata tag — the union of the Content Description, Extended Content Description, Metadata, and Metadata Library objects discovered inside the Header Object. Objects Gets every ASF object encountered by the walker, in file order. The first entry is always the Header Object itself; the remainder are its direct children. Nested children of the Header Extension Object (Metadata, Metadata Library) are not exposed here. PlayDuration100Ns Gets the file's Play Duration as reported by the File Properties Object, in 100-nanosecond units. 0 if the File Properties Object is absent. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods Dispose() Releases the underlying ISourceReader. Does not close the user's source System.IO.Stream; the caller still owns that. ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.BitStream.Read.html.title: Method Read | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.BitStream.Read.html.summary: Method Read Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Read(byte[], int, int) When overridden in a derived class, reads a sequence of bytes from the current stream and advances the position within the stream by the number of bytes read. public override int Read(byte[] buffer, int offset, int count) Parameters buffer byte[] An array of bytes. When this method returns, the buffer contains the specified byte array with the values between offset and (offset + count - 1) replaced by the bytes read from the current source. offset int The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin storing the data read from the current stream. count int The maximum number of bytes to be read from the current stream. Returns int The total number of bytes read into the buffer. This can be less than the number of bytes requested if that many bytes are not currently available, or zero (0) if count is 0 or the end of the stream has been reached. Exceptions ArgumentException The sum of offset and count is larger than the buffer length. ArgumentNullException buffer is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException offset or count is negative. IOException An I/O error occurs. NotSupportedException The stream does not support reading. ObjectDisposedException Methods were called after the stream was closed.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.DffStream.html.title: Class DffStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.DffStream.html.summary: Class DffStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for Philips DFF (Direct Stream Digital Interchange File Format) DSD audio containers (.dff). Does not decode audio samples. public sealed class DffStream : IMediaContainer Inheritance object DffStream Implements IMediaContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker captures top-level chunks inside the FRM8 form (form-type "DSD "), descends into the PROP form to extract FS (sample rate) and CHNL (channel count), and locates optional DIIN, COMT, and ID3 chunks. All multi-byte integers in DFF are big-endian. Properties BitsPerSample Gets the bits-per-sample value (DSD is always 1). ChannelIds Gets the channel identifiers from PROP/CHNL, one four-CC per channel. Channels Gets the channel count from PROP/CHNL. Chunks Gets the top-level chunks discovered inside the FRM8 form, in file order. ComtChunk Gets the raw payload of the optional COMT comments chunk, or null when absent. DataOffset Gets the absolute offset of the DSD audio chunk's payload, or 0 if the chunk is absent. DataSize Gets the size of the DSD audio chunk's payload in bytes. DiinChunk Gets the raw payload of the optional DIIN info chunk, or null when absent. EmbeddedId3v2 Gets the embedded ID3v2 tag from the optional ID3 chunk, or null when absent or unparseable. EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IMediaContainer, where it ends in the stream. FormatType Gets the form-type four-CC (always "DSD " for a recognised DFF file). FormatVersion Gets the format version from the FVER chunk (high byte = major). MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. SampleCount Gets the per-channel sample count, computed from the DSD audio chunk size and channel count. SampleRate Gets the sample rate from PROP/FS , in Hz. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. ToByteArray() WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.DsfStream.html.title: Class DsfStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.DsfStream.html.summary: Class DsfStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for Sony DSF (Direct Stream File) DSD audio containers (.dsf). Does not decode audio samples. public sealed class DsfStream : IMediaContainer Inheritance object DsfStream Implements IMediaContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker captures the DSD , fmt , and data chunks (in that order, per spec) and locates an optional ID3v2 metadata block at the absolute offset given by the DSD chunk's metadata-pointer field. All multi-byte integers in DSF are little-endian. Properties BitsPerSample Gets the bits-per-sample value from the fmt chunk (1 or 8). BlockSizePerChannel Gets the per-channel block size from the fmt chunk (typically 4096). ChannelType Gets the channel-type code from the fmt chunk (1=mono, 2=stereo, 3=3ch, 4=quad, 5=4ch, 6=5ch, 7=5.1ch). Channels Gets the channel count from the fmt chunk. Chunks Gets the chunks discovered inside the DSF container, in file order. DataOffset Gets the absolute offset of the data chunk's payload, or 0 if the chunk is absent. DataSize Gets the size of the data chunk's payload in bytes (excludes the 12-byte chunk header). DeclaredFileSize Gets the declared total file size from the DSD chunk, in bytes. EmbeddedId3v2 Gets the embedded ID3v2 tag, or null when no metadata block is present or it could not be parsed. EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IMediaContainer, where it ends in the stream. FormatId Gets the format identifier from the fmt chunk (0 = DSD raw). FormatVersion Gets the format version from the fmt chunk (typically 1). MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. MetadataPointer Gets the absolute offset of the embedded ID3v2 metadata block, or 0 if no metadata is declared. SampleCount Gets the per-channel sample count from the fmt chunk. SampleRate Gets the sample rate from the fmt chunk, in Hz (e.g. 2822400 for DSD64). StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. ToByteArray() WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.ISourceReader.html.title: Interface ISourceReader | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.ISourceReader.html.summary: Interface ISourceReader Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Random-access read facade over the bytes a media-container walker captured at parse time. public interface ISourceReader : IDisposable Inherited Members IDisposable.Dispose() Remarks Splice rewriters (MP4, ASF, Matroska, DSF, DFF) need to copy unchanged byte ranges from the source at write time. Holding the entire input in memory does not scale to multi-GB files; ISourceReader abstracts the random-access read so the same walker can run against either an in-memory buffer (MemorySourceReader) or a seekable file stream (StreamSourceReader). Implementations are not required to be thread-safe — a walker should hold a single reader and use it sequentially. Callers must keep the underlying source (file stream, memory buffer) alive until the walker is done with it; IDisposable is implemented so implementations can release their own resources, but the caller's source is not owned. Properties Length Gets the total length of the source, in bytes. Methods CopyTo(long, long, Stream) Copies a byte range from the source directly to destination. Read(long, Span<byte>) Reads bytes starting at offset into destination. Reads exactly destination.Length bytes; throws System.IO.EndOfStreamException if fewer are available.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MatroskaStream.html.title: Class MatroskaStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MatroskaStream.html.summary: Class MatroskaStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Walks a Matroska / WebM (EBML) container looking for the Tags element of the first segment. Does not decode codec payload from Cluster children. public sealed class MatroskaStream : IMediaContainer, IDisposable Inheritance object MatroskaStream Implements IMediaContainer IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks Supports round-trip serialisation: the source bytes are kept reachable via an ISourceReader reference (no full-file copy in memory), and a new Tags element is spliced in (or appended) at WriteTo(Stream) time by streaming unchanged regions directly from the source. This is what makes editing a 40 GB MKV viable: peak memory stays in the low MB range regardless of file size. Callers must keep the underlying source (the System.IO.Stream passed to ReadStream(Stream)) alive until WriteTo(Stream) / ToByteArray finishes — the walker reads from it on demand. TracksId Tracks element id. Properties DocType Gets the EBML DocType declared in the header (e.g. "matroska", "webm"). Duration Gets the raw Duration value (in TimecodeScale units), or 0 if absent. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. Tag Gets the parsed Tags element, aggregating all top-level Tag entries from the first segment. TimecodeScale Gets the segment TimecodeScale in nanoseconds per tick (default 1,000,000 = 1ms). TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MediaContainers.Max.title: Property MaxStreamSpacingLength | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MediaContainers.Max.summary: Property MaxStreamSpacingLength Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MaxStreamSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between containers when searching the stream. public int MaxStreamSpacingLength { get; set; } Property Value int The max length of spacing, in bytes. Remarks Larger values cost more in scan time but tolerate more junk between containers. Set to Strict (= 0) to skip the byte-by-byte rescan entirely — the reader still uses the magic-byte fast path, so well-formed FLAC / OGG / WAV / MP4 / ASF / Matroska / DSF / DFF files dispatch in O(1). Useful when you know the input is clean; combine with AudioTags's strict mode to scan a whole file in near-constant time.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.Mp4Stream.WriteTo.h.title: Method WriteTo | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.Mp4Stream.WriteTo.h.summary: Method WriteTo Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination. public void WriteTo(Stream destination) Parameters destination Stream The stream to write the container bytes into. Remarks Streams head + new ilst + tail directly from the source to the destination, patching the size fields of the enclosing moov / udta / meta boxes inline as the bytes flow through. Peak memory is bounded by the metadata size, not the file size. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If destination is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.Mp4Stream.html.title: Class Mp4Stream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.Mp4Stream.html.summary: Class Mp4Stream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for ISO/IEC 14496-12 (MP4 / M4A / 3GP / MOV) containers. Surfaces the iTunes-style metadata in moov.udta.meta.ilst and reports duration from moov.mvhd. public sealed class Mp4Stream : IMediaContainer, IDisposable Inheritance object Mp4Stream Implements IMediaContainer IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker keeps an ISourceReader reference rather than buffering the file in memory; WriteTo(Stream) streams unchanged regions directly from source to destination, so editing iTunes metadata in a multi-GB MP4 is bounded by the metadata size, not the file size. Box sizes are bound-checked against the parent; size==0 ("to end of file") and size==1 ("64-bit extended size in the next 8 bytes") are both supported. Recursion is bounded by an explicit depth cap to avoid stack blow-up on malicious input. Callers must keep the source System.IO.Stream passed to ReadStream(Stream) alive until WriteTo(Stream) / ToByteArray finishes. Properties Boxes Gets the top-level boxes discovered in the container, in file order. EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IMediaContainer, where it ends in the stream. MajorBrand Gets the four-character major brand reported by the ftyp box, or Empty if absent. MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. Tag Gets the parsed iTunes metadata tag, or an empty tag if none was present. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods Dispose() Releases the underlying ISourceReader. Does not close the user's source System.IO.Stream; the caller still owns that. ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MpaStream.TotalDura.title: Property TotalDuration | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.MpaStream.TotalDura.summary:
Property TotalDuration Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. public long TotalDuration { get; } Property Value long The total length of audio, in milliseconds. Examples // This method does a rough estimation of audio length of a CBR audio file. public long EstimateFrameLengthCBR() { FileInfo file = new FileInfo("audio.mp3"); using (FileStream fileStream = file.OpenRead()) { MPAStream mpaStream = MPAStream.ReadStream(fileStream);

// You can only use this method on CBR files, not on those that have a VBR header included. if ((mpaStream.vbrHeader != null) || (mpaStream.FirstFrame == null)) return 0;

MPAFrame frame = mpaStream.FirstFrame;

// Calculate the audio size, in bits per second. long totalAudioSize = TotalMediaSize * 8;

// Return the divided total audio size by the bitrate of the first frame, // this results in a roughly estimated audio length in milliseconds. return Convert.ToInt64(totalAudioSize / (float)frame.Bitrate); } } // This method does a rough estimation of audio length of a VBR audio file. public long EstimateFrameLengthVBR() { FileInfo file = new FileInfo("audio.mp3"); using (FileStream fileStream = file.OpenRead()) { MPAStream mpaStream = MPAStream.ReadStream(fileStream);

// You can only use this method on VBR files, not on CBR files because those don't have a VBR header. if ((mpaStream.vbrHeader == null) || (mpaStream.FirstFrame == null)) return 0;

VBRHeader vbrHeader = mpaStream.VbrHeader;

// Store the frame count from the VBR header. long frameCount = vbrHeader.FrameCount;

// If the VBRHeader is a XING header, we need to add one frame to the frame count, // as the frame count in the XING header excludes it's own frame. if (vbrHeader.HeaderType == VBRHeaderType.XING) frameCount++;

MPAFrame firstFrame = mpaStream.FirstFrame;

// Return the length of the first frame multiplied with the total amount of frames in the stream, // as indicated by the VBR header. return frameCount * firstFrame.AudioLength; } } Remarks It calculates the audio length by adding up the audio length of each frame. Note that this method is slow but accurate. A faster but less accurate method is to calculate the total file size in bits per second, and dividing this by the bitrate.
