api/AudioVideoLib.IO.OggStream.html.summary: Class OggStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Walks an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533) page-by-page. Does not decode Vorbis/Opus/Theora payload. public sealed class OggStream : IMediaContainer Inheritance object OggStream Implements IMediaContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker records structural metadata for every page and peeks at the first beginning-of-stream page to identify the codec (Vorbis or Opus) so it can report channel count, sample rate, and an approximate duration derived from the largest observed granule position. api/AudioVideoLib.IO.RiffStream.html.title: Class RiffStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.RiffStream.html.summary: Class RiffStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Minimal structural walker for RIFF containers (e.g. WAV and RIFX). Does not decode audio samples. public sealed class RiffStream : IMediaContainer Inheritance object RiffStream Implements IMediaContainer Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Extension Methods IMediaContainerExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.ToByteArray(IMediaContainer) IMediaContainerExtensions.TryWriteTo(IMediaContainer, Span<byte>, out int) IMediaContainerExtensions.WriteTo(IMediaContainer, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks The walker captures the fmt and data chunks needed to report format parameters and locate the sample block, and records every other chunk structurally without materialising its payload. Only the WAVE form type is recognised; other RIFF types such as AVI are ignored. Properties AudioFormat Gets the WAV wFormatTag value from the fmt chunk (e.g. 1 = PCM, 3 = IEEE float). BextChunk Gets the parsed BWF bext chunk, or null if absent or malformed. BitsPerSample Gets the bits-per-sample value from the fmt chunk. BlockAlign Gets the block alignment from the fmt chunk. ByteRate Gets the average byte rate from the fmt chunk. Channels Gets the channel count from the fmt chunk. Chunks Gets the chunks discovered inside the RIFF container, in file order. DataOffset Gets the absolute offset of the data chunk's payload, or 0 if the chunk is absent. DataSize Gets the size of the data chunk's payload in bytes. EmbeddedId3v2 Gets the parsed ID3v2 tag from an embedded id3 (or ID3 ) chunk, or null. EndOffset Gets the end offset of the IMediaContainer, where it ends in the stream. FormatType Gets the four-character RIFF form type. Only "WAVE" is currently recognised. InfoTag Gets the parsed LIST INFO tag if one was present, otherwise null. When multiple LIST INFO chunks are present, items are merged in file order. IxmlChunk Gets the parsed iXML chunk, or null if absent or empty. MaxFrameSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between 2 frames when searching for frames. SampleRate Gets the sample rate from the fmt chunk, in Hz. StartOffset Gets the start offset of the IMediaContainer, where it starts in the stream. TotalDuration Gets the total length of audio in milliseconds. TotalMediaSize Gets the total size of audio data in bytes. Methods ReadStream(Stream) Reads the audio stream from the stream. WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this container to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.BeginR.title: Method BeginRead | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.BeginR.summary: Method BeginRead Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll BeginRead(byte[], int, int, AsyncCallback?, object?) Begins an asynchronous read operation. (Consider using ReadAsync(byte[], int, int) instead.) public override IAsyncResult BeginRead(byte[] buffer, int offset, int count, AsyncCallback? callback, object? state) Parameters buffer byte[] The buffer to read the data into. offset int The byte offset in buffer at which to begin writing data read from the stream. count int The maximum number of bytes to read. callback AsyncCallback An optional asynchronous callback, to be called when the read is complete. state object A user-provided object that distinguishes this particular asynchronous read request from other requests. Returns IAsyncResult An IAsyncResult that represents the asynchronous read, which could still be pending. Exceptions IOException Attempted an asynchronous read past the end of the stream, or a disk error occurs. ArgumentException One or more of the arguments is invalid. ObjectDisposedException Methods were called after the stream was closed. NotSupportedException The current Stream implementation does not support the read operation.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.BeginW.summary: Method BeginWrite Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll BeginWrite(byte[], int, int, AsyncCallback?, object?) Returns IAsyncResult An IAsyncResult that represents the asynchronous write, which could still be pending. Exceptions IOException Attempted an asynchronous write past the end of the stream, or a disk error occurs. ArgumentException One or more of the arguments is invalid. ObjectDisposedException Methods were called after the stream was closed. NotSupportedException The current Stream implementation does not support the write operation.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.EndRea.title: Method EndRead | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.EndRea.summary: Method EndRead Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EndRead(IAsyncResult) Waits for the pending asynchronous read to complete. (Consider using ReadAsync(byte[], int, int) instead.) public override int EndRead(IAsyncResult asyncResult) Parameters asyncResult IAsyncResult The reference to the pending asynchronous request to finish. Returns int The number of bytes read from the stream, between zero (0) and the number of bytes requested. ReadAsync returns zero (0) only if zero bytes were requested or if no more bytes will be available because it's at the end of the stream; otherwise, read operations do not complete until at least one byte is available. If zero bytes are requested, read operations may complete immediately or may not complete until at least one byte is available (but without consuming any data). Exceptions ArgumentNullException asyncResult is null. ArgumentException A handle to the pending read operation is not available. -or- The pending operation does not support reading. InvalidOperationException asyncResult did not originate from a BeginRead(byte[], int, int, AsyncCallback, object) method on the current stream. IOException The stream is closed or an internal error has occurred.

InvalidOperationException asyncResult did not originate from a BeginWrite(byte[], int, int, AsyncCallback, object) method on the current stream. IOException The stream is closed or an internal error has occurred.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.Read.h.title: Method Read | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamBuffer.Read.h.summary: Method Read Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Read(byte[], int) Read specified amount of bytes from the stream, and stores it into the given buffer. public int Read(byte[] buffer, int count) Parameters buffer byte[] The buffer to store the read data in. count int The maximum number of bytes to be read from the stream. Returns int The amount of bytes written into the buffer. This can be less than the number of bytes requested if that number of bytes are not currently available, or zero if the end of the stream is reached before any bytes are read. Read(byte[], int, int) When overridden in a derived class, reads a sequence of bytes from the current stream and advances the position within the stream by the number of bytes read. public override int Read(byte[] buffer, int offset, int count) Parameters buffer byte[] An array of bytes. When this method returns, the buffer contains the specified byte array with the values between offset and (offset + count - 1) replaced by the bytes read from the current source. offset int The zero-based byte offset in buffer at which to begin storing the data read from the current stream. count int The maximum number of bytes to be read from the current stream. Returns int The total number of bytes read into the buffer. This can be less than the number of bytes requested if that many bytes are not currently available, or zero (0) if count is 0 or the end of the stream has been reached. Exceptions ArgumentException The sum of offset and count is larger than the buffer length. ArgumentNullException buffer is null. ArgumentOutOfRangeException offset or count is negative. IOException An I/O error occurs. NotSupportedException The stream does not support reading. ObjectDisposedException Methods were called after the stream was closed.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamSourceReader..title: Class StreamSourceReader | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.StreamSourceReader..summary: Class StreamSourceReader Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ISourceReader implementation backed by a seekable System.IO.Stream. public sealed class StreamSourceReader : ISourceReader, IDisposable Inheritance object StreamSourceReader Implements ISourceReader IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The stream is borrowed by default — disposal does not close it unless leaveOpen was set to false at construction. The caller is responsible for ensuring the stream remains valid for the lifetime of any walker that holds this reader. Calls are not thread-safe; the reader uses System.IO.Stream.Position directly and assumes serial access. Constructors StreamSourceReader(Stream, bool) Initializes a new StreamSourceReader over a seekable stream. Properties Length Gets the total length of the source, in bytes. Methods Dispose() Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources. Read(long, Span<byte>) Reads bytes starting at offset into destination. Reads exactly destination.Length bytes; throws System.IO.EndOfStreamException if fewer are available.

api/AudioVideoLib.IO.html.title: Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.IO.html.summary: Namespace AudioVideoLib.IO Classes AiffStream Minimal structural walker for AIFF and AIFF-C containers. Does not decode audio samples. AsfStream Minimal structural walker for ASF (Advanced Systems Format) containers — i.e. .asf, .wma, and .wmv files. Does not decode media payload. BitStream Wrapper class around a stream to use as backing data with various ways to read and write. DffStream Minimal structural walker for Philips DFF (Direct Stream Digital Interchange File Format) DSD audio containers (.dff). Does not decode audio samples. DsfStream Minimal structural walker for Sony DSF (Direct Stream File) DSD audio containers (.dsf). Does not decode audio samples. FlacStream The stream containing FLAC Audio FlacFrames. IMediaContainerExtensions Buffer-shaped convenience helpers around WriteTo(Stream). MatroskaStream Walks a Matroska / WebM (EBML) container looking for the Tags element of the first segment. Does not decode codec payload from Cluster children. MediaContainerParseEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. MediaContainerParsedEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. MediaContainers Represents a collection of audio streams. MemorySourceReader ISourceReader implementation backed by an in-memory byte array. Mp4Stream Minimal structural walker for ISO/IEC 14496-12 (MP4 / M4A / 3GP / MOV) containers. Surfaces the iTunes-style metadata in moov.udta.meta.ilst and reports duration from moov.mvhd. MpaStream The stream containing MPEG Audio MpaFrames. OggStream Walks an Ogg bitstream (RFC 3533) page-by-page. Does not decode Vorbis/Opus/Theora payload. RiffStream Minimal structural walker for RIFF containers (e.g. WAV and RIFX). Does not decode audio samples. StreamBuffer Wrapper class around a stream to use as backing data with various ways to read and write. StreamSourceReader ISourceReader implementation backed by a seekable System.IO.Stream. Interfaces IMediaContainer Interface for an audio format to implement streaming. ISourceReader Random-access read facade over the bytes a media-container walker captured at parse time. IStreamOffset Defines the offset of an item in a System.IO.Stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.ApeItemType.html.title: Enum ApeItemType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.ApeItemType.html.summary: Enum ApeItemType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Defines the type of an ApeItem. public enum ApeItemType Fields CodedUTF8 = 0 Item contains text information coded in UTF-8. ContainsBinary = 1 Item contains binary information. Binary information: Information which should not be edited by a text editor, because: Information is not a text; Contains control characters; Contains internal restrictions which can't be handled by a normal text editor; Can't be easily interpreted by humans; IsLocator = 2 Item is a locator of external stored information. Allowed formats: http://host/directory/filename.ext ftp://host/directory/filename.ext filename.ext /directory/filename.ext DRIVE:/directory/filename.ext Note: Locators are also UTF-8 encoded. This can especially occur when filenames are encoded. Reserved = 3 Reserved type.

Unknown = 0 Failure type couldn't be classified — fall back to the raw exception. UnsupportedVersion = 3 The tag declared a version the library does not support.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.AudioTags.MaxTagS.title: Property MaxTagSpacingLength | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.AudioTags.MaxTagS.summary: Property MaxTagSpacingLength Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MaxTagSpacingLength Gets or sets the max length of spacing, in bytes, between two tags when searching the stream. public int MaxTagSpacingLength { get; set; } Property Value int The max length of spacing, in bytes. Remarks Larger values cost more in scan time but tolerate more junk between tags. Set to Strict (= 0) to skip the byte-by-byte rescan entirely — the reader will only check the canonical anchor positions (start or end of stream depending on TagOrigin). Recommended for clean, well-formed input where you control how files were written.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v1Tag.WriteTo..title: Method WriteTo | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v1Tag.WriteTo..summary: Method WriteTo Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this tag to the supplied destination. public void WriteTo(Stream destination) Parameters destination Stream The stream to write the tag bytes into. Remarks The extended tags will only be written when UseExtendedTag is set to true. Field bytes are written verbatim from the per-field raw byte storage, so the per-field Encoding chosen at parse (or set via ...Encoding properties) round-trips faithfully. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If destination is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v1Tag.html.title: Class Id3v1Tag | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v1Tag.html.summary: Class Id3v1Tag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Per-field text storage for an ID3v1 tag. The source of truth is the raw byte sequence captured at parse time; a per-field Encoding override controls how those bytes are decoded and (on save) how new string values are re-encoded. This lets callers display a tag written with one encoding (e.g. UTF-8) even when the tag-level default is something else (e.g. Latin-1). public sealed class Id3v1Tag : IAudioTag, IEquatable<IAudioTag> Inheritance object Id3v1Tag Implements IAudioTag IEquatable<IAudioTag> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods IAudioTagExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IAudioTag) IAudioTagExtensions.ToByteArray(IAudioTag) IAudioTagExtensions.TryWriteTo(IAudioTag, Span<byte>, out int) IAudioTagExtensions.WriteTo(IAudioTag, IBufferWriter<byte>) Constructors Id3v1Tag() Initializes a new instance of the Id3v1Tag class. Id3v1Tag(Id3v1Version) Initializes a new instance of the Id3v1Tag class. Fields ExtendedHeaderIdentifier The extended header identifier for an Id3v1Tag. ExtendedSize The extended size of an Id3v1Tag. HeaderIdentifier The header identifier for an Id3v1Tag. TotalSize The total size of an Id3v1Tag. Properties AlbumTitle Gets or sets the album title string. Decoded from AlbumTitleRawBytes via EffectiveAlbumTitleEncoding; assigning re-encodes the raw bytes, truncated to the spec maximum (30 bytes standard, 90 bytes when UseExtendedTag). AlbumTitleEncoding Per-field encoding override for AlbumTitle; null falls back to Encoding. AlbumTitleRawBytes Raw bytes for the album field — combined standard (30) + extended (60) regions if extended is in use. AlbumYear Gets or sets the album year string (4 bytes ASCII). Decoded from AlbumYearRawBytes via EffectiveAlbumYearEncoding; assigning truncates to 4 bytes. AlbumYearEncoding Per-field encoding override for AlbumYear; null falls back to Encoding. AlbumYearRawBytes Raw bytes for the year field (4 bytes max). Artist Gets or sets the artist string. Decoded from ArtistRawBytes via EffectiveArtistEncoding; assigning re-encodes the raw bytes, truncated to the spec maximum (30 bytes standard, 90 bytes when UseExtendedTag). ArtistEncoding Per-field encoding override for Artist; null falls back to Encoding. ArtistRawBytes Raw bytes for the artist field — combined standard (30) + extended (60) regions if extended is in use. EffectiveAlbumTitleEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding AlbumTitle. EffectiveAlbumYearEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding AlbumYear. EffectiveArtistEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding Artist. EffectiveExtendedTrackGenreEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding ExtendedTrackGenre. EffectiveTrackCommentEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding TrackComment. EffectiveTrackTitleEncoding Effective encoding used when decoding/encoding TrackTitle. Encoding Tag-level fallback Encoding. Used for any field whose own per-field Encoding property is null. EndTime Gets or sets the end time (TAG+ only). ExtendedTrackGenre Gets or sets the extended track genre string (TAG+ only). Decoded from ExtendedTrackGenreRawBytes via EffectiveExtendedTrackGenreEncoding. ExtendedTrackGenreEncoding Per-field encoding override for ExtendedTrackGenre; null falls back to Encoding. ExtendedTrackGenreRawBytes Raw bytes for the extended genre field (TAG+, 30 bytes max). Genre Gets or sets the genre. StartTime Gets or sets the start time (TAG+ only). TrackComment Gets or sets the track comment string. Decoded from TrackCommentRawBytes via EffectiveTrackCommentEncoding; assigning truncates to TrackCommentLength bytes. TrackCommentEncoding Per-field encoding override for TrackComment; null falls back to Encoding. TrackCommentLength Gets the length of the track comment region for the current version (28 for v1.1, 30 for v1.0). TrackCommentRawBytes Raw bytes for the comment field (28 bytes for v1.1, 30 bytes for v1.0). TrackNumber Gets or sets the track number (1-byte unsigned). Only meaningful for Id3v11. TrackSpeed Gets or sets the track speed (TAG+ only). TrackTitle Gets or sets the title string. Decoded from TrackTitleRawBytes via EffectiveTrackTitleEncoding; assigning re-encodes the raw bytes, truncated to the spec maximum (30 bytes standard, 90 bytes when UseExtendedTag). TrackTitleEncoding Per-field encoding override for TrackTitle; null falls back to Encoding. TrackTitleRawBytes Raw bytes for the title field — combined standard (30) + extended (60) regions if extended is in use. UseExtendedTag Gets or sets a value indicating whether to write the extended TAG+ block. Version Gets the Id3v1Version. Methods Equals(IAudioTag?) Indicates whether the current object is equal to another object of the same type. Equals(Id3v1Tag?) Equals the specified Id3v1Tag. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object. GetHashCode() Serves as a hash function for a particular type. IsValidGenre(Id3v1Genre) Determines whether the specified genre is valid. IsValidTrackSpeed(Id3v1TrackSpeed) Determines whether the specified track is valid. IsValidVersion(Id3v1Version) Determines whether the specified version is valid. ToString() Returns a string that represents the current object. WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised form of this tag to the supplied destination.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AdjustmentPo.summary: Property Frequency Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Frequency Gets the frequency. public short Frequency { get; } Property Value short Remarks The frequency is stored in units of 1/2 Hz, giving it a range from 0 to 32767 Hz.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AdjustmentPo.title: Property VolumeAdjustment | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AdjustmentPo.summary: Property VolumeAdjustment Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll VolumeAdjustment Gets the volume adjustment. public short VolumeAdjustment { get; } Property Value short Remarks The volume adjustment is encoded as a fixed point decibel value, 16 bit signed integer representing (adjustment * 512), giving +/- 64 dB with a precision of 0.001953125 dB. E.g. +2 dB is stored as 0x04 0x00 and -2 dB is 0xFC 0x00.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AttachedPict.title: Property Description | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AttachedPict.summary: Property Description Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Description Gets or sets the description. public string Description { get; set; } Property Value string The description. Remarks Description is a short description of the picture, represented as a terminated text string. In versions before Id3v240, the description has a maximum length of 64 characters, but may be empty. If the value is longer than 64 character the value will be trimmed. New lines are not allowed.

Equals the specified Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame. public bool Equals(Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame? ap) Parameters ap Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame The Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame. Returns bool true if equal; false otherwise. Remarks Both instances are equal when their Version and Description properties are equal (case-insensitive), or, when their Version property is equal and both use FileIcon or OtherFileIcon as PictureType.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.title: Property OwnerIdentifier | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.summary: Property OwnerIdentifier Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OwnerIdentifier Gets or sets the owner identifier. public string OwnerIdentifier { get; set; } Property Value string The owner identifier. Remarks The owner identifier is a terminated string with a URL containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted audio file. Questions regarding the encrypted audio should be sent to the email address specified. If the owner identifier is empty and the audio file indeed is encrypted, the whole file may be considered useless. The URL must be a valid RFC 1738 URL, use IsValidUrl(string) to check if a value is a valid URL.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.title: Property PreviewLength | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.summary: Property PreviewLength Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PreviewLength Gets or sets the length of the preview. public short PreviewLength { get; set; } Property Value short The length of the preview. Remarks The PreviewStart and preview length are described in frames. If no part is unencrypted, these fields should be left zeroed.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.title: Property PreviewStart | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.summary: Property PreviewStart Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PreviewStart Gets or sets the preview start. public short PreviewStart { get; set; } Property Value short The preview start. Remarks The preview start is a pointer to an unencrypted part of the audio. The preview start and PreviewLength are described in frames. If no part is unencrypted, these fields should be left zeroed.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioEncrypt.summary: Class Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Class for storing audio encryption. public sealed class Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame : Id3v2Frame, IAudioTagFrame, IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame>, IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inheritance object Id3v2Frame Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame Implements IAudioTagFrame IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame> IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inherited Members Id3v2Frame.MaxAllowedSize Id3v2Frame.EncryptedData Id3v2Frame.CompressedData Id3v2Frame.Cryptor Id3v2Frame.Compressor Id3v2Frame.Equals(object) Id3v2Frame.Equals(IAudioTagFrame) Id3v2Frame.GetHashCode() Id3v2Frame.SetVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Decrypt() Id3v2Frame.Decompress() Id3v2Frame.ToByteArray() Id3v2Frame.IsValidEncodingType(Id3v2FrameEncodingType) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifier<T>(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Version Id3v2Frame.EncryptionType Id3v2Frame.GroupIdentifier Id3v2Frame.TagAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.FileAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.IsReadOnly Id3v2Frame.UseCompression Id3v2Frame.UseEncryption Id3v2Frame.UseGroupingIdentity Id3v2Frame.UseUnsynchronization Id3v2Frame.UseDataLengthIndicator Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifier(Id3v2Version, string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidLanguageCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCountryCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCurrencyCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidUrl(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidDefaultTextString(string, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTextString(string, Id3v2FrameEncodingType, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTimeStampFormat(Id3v2TimeStampFormat) Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifierByte(short) Id3v2Frame.IsValidVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifierFieldLength(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream<T>(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This frame indicates if the actual audio stream is encrypted, and by whom. Since standardization of such encryption scheme is beyond this document, all Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame frames contain an URL which can be an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted audio file. Questions regarding the encrypted audio should be sent to the email address specified. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to and including Id3v240. api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2AudioSeekPoi.summary: Property BitsPerIndexPoint Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll BitsPerIndexPoint Gets or sets the bits per index point. public byte BitsPerIndexPoint { get; set; } Property Value byte The bits per index point. Remarks Bits per index point is 8 or 16, depending on the chosen precision. 8 bits works well for short files (less than 5 minutes of audio), while 16 bits is advantageous for long files.

sealed class Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame : Id3v2Frame, IAudioTagFrame, IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame>, IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inheritance object Id3v2Frame Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame Implements IAudioTagFrame IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame> IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inherited Members Id3v2Frame.MaxAllowedSize Id3v2Frame.EncryptedData Id3v2Frame.CompressedData Id3v2Frame.Cryptor Id3v2Frame.Compressor Id3v2Frame.IsValidEncodingType(Id3v2FrameEncodingType) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifier<T>(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Version Id3v2Frame.EncryptionType Id3v2Frame.GroupIdentifier Id3v2Frame.TagAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.FileAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.IsReadOnly Id3v2Frame.UseCompression Id3v2Frame.UseEncryption Id3v2Frame.UseGroupingIdentity Id3v2Frame.UseUnsynchronization Id3v2Frame.UseDataLengthIndicator Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifier(Id3v2Version, string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidLanguageCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCountryCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCurrencyCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidUrl(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidDefaultTextString(string, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTextString(string, Id3v2FrameEncodingType, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTimeStampFormat(Id3v2TimeStampFormat) Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifierByte(short) Id3v2Frame.IsValidVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifierFieldLength(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream<T>(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Audio files with variable bitrates are intrinsically difficult to deal with in the case of seeking within the file. The Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame frame makes seeking easier by providing a list a seek points within the audio file. The seek points are a fractional offset within the audio data, providing a starting point from which to find an appropriate point to start decoding. The presence of an Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame frame requires the existence of the Length property, indicating the duration of the file in milliseconds. This frame supports Id3v2Version Id3v240 only. Constructors Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame() Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame class with version Id3v240. Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame(Id3v2Version) Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame class. Properties BitsPerIndexPoint Gets or sets the bits per index point. Data Gets the data and only the data of this frame. FractionAtIndex Gets the fraction at an index. Identifier Gets the identifier of this frame. IndexedDataLength Gets or sets the length of the indexed data. IndexedDataStart Gets or sets the indexed data start. NumberOfIndexPoints Gets or sets the number of index points. Methods Equals(Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame?) Equals the specified Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame. Equals(Id3v2Frame?) Equals the specified Id3v2Frame. IsVersionSupported(Id3v2Version) Determines whether the specified version is supported by the frame.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ChannelInfor.summary: Property PeakVolume Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PeakVolume Gets the peak volume. public long PeakVolume { get; } Property Value long Remarks The peak volume field is always padded to whole bytes, setting the most significant bits to zero.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ChannelInfor.title: Property VolumeAdjustment | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ChannelInfor.summary: Property VolumeAdjustment Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll VolumeAdjustment Gets the volume adjustment. public float VolumeAdjustment { get; } Property Value float Remarks The volume adjustment is encoded as a fixed point decibel value, 16 bit signed integer representing (adjustment * 512), giving +/- 64 dB with a precision of 0.001953125 dB. E.g. +2 dB is stored as 0x04 0x00 and -2 dB is 0xFC 0x00.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ContentType..summary: Enum Id3v2ContentType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Possible values the content type can be. public enum Id3v2ContentType Fields Chord = 5 Indicates Chord (e.g. "Bb F"). Events = 4 Indicates Events (e.g. "Don Did enters the stage"). ImagesUrls = 8 Indicates URLs to images. This value has been added as of Id3v240 and should not be used for earlier versions. Lyrics = 1 Indicates Lyrics. MovementName = 3 Indicates a Movement/part name (e.g. "Adagio"). Other = 0 Indicates Other content type. TextTranscription = 2 Indicates Text transcription. Trivia = 6 Indicates Trivia/'pop up' information. This value has been added as of Id3v230 and should not be used for earlier versions. WebpagesUrls = 7 Indicates URLs to webpages. This value has been added as of Id3v240 and should not be used for earlier versions.

Remarks The owner identifier is terminated string with a URL [URL] containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted meta frame. Questions regarding the encrypted frame should be sent to the indicated email address. If the owner identifier is empty, the whole frame should be ignored, and preferably be removed. The URL must be a valid RFC 1738 URL, use IsValidUrl(string) to check if a value is a valid URL.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2EncryptedMet.summary: Class Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Class for storing an Id3v2 encrypted meta frame. public sealed class Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame : Id3v2Frame, IAudioTagFrame, IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame>, IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inheritance object Id3v2Frame Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame Implements IAudioTagFrame IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame> IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inherited Members Id3v2Frame.MaxAllowedSize Id3v2Frame.EncryptedData Id3v2Frame.CompressedData Id3v2Frame.Cryptor Id3v2Frame.Compressor Id3v2Frame.Equals(object) Id3v2Frame.Equals(IAudioTagFrame) Id3v2Frame.GetHashCode() Id3v2Frame.SetVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Decrypt() Id3v2Frame.Decompress() Id3v2Frame.ToByteArray() Id3v2Frame.IsValidEncodingType(Id3v2FrameEncodingType) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifier<T>(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Version Id3v2Frame.EncryptionType Id3v2Frame.GroupIdentifier Id3v2Frame.TagAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.FileAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.IsReadOnly Id3v2Frame.UseCompression Id3v2Frame.UseEncryption Id3v2Frame.UseGroupingIdentity Id3v2Frame.UseUnsynchronization Id3v2Frame.UseDataLengthIndicator Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifier(Id3v2Version, string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidLanguageCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCountryCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCurrencyCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidUrl(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidDefaultTextString(string, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTextString(string, Id3v2FrameEncodingType, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTimeStampFormat(Id3v2TimeStampFormat) Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifierByte(short) Id3v2Frame.IsValidVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifierFieldLength(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream<T>(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This frame contains one or more encrypted frames. This enables protection of copyrighted information such as pictures and text, that people might want to pay extra for. Since standardization of such an encryption scheme is beyond this document, all Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame frames contain an URL [URL] which can be an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted meta frame. Questions regarding the encrypted frame should be sent to the indicated email address. When an Id3v2 decoder encounters an Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame frame, it should send the data block to the 'plugin' with the corresponding OwnerIdentifier and expect to receive either a data block with one or several Id3v2 frames after each other or an error. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to but not including Id3v230. Constructors Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame() Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame class with version Id3v220. Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame(Id3v2Version) Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame class. Properties ContentExplanation Gets or sets the content explanation. Data Gets the data and only the data of this frame. EncryptedDataBlock Gets or sets the encrypted data block. Identifier Gets the identifier of this frame. OwnerIdentifier Gets or sets the owner identifier. Methods Equals(Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame?) Equals the specified Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame. Equals(Id3v2Frame?) Equals the specified Id3v2Frame. IsVersionSupported(Id3v2Version) Determines whether the specified version is supported by the frame.

Remarks The owner identifier is a null-terminated string with a URL containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encryption method. Questions regarding the encryption method should be sent to the indicated email address. The URL must be a valid RFC 1738 URL, use IsValidUrl(string) to check if a value is a valid URL.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ExtendedHead.title: Method GetHeaderSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ExtendedHead.summary: Method GetHeaderSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll GetHeaderSize(Id3v2Version) Gets the size of the extended header for the specified Id3v2Version. public int GetHeaderSize(Id3v2Version version) Parameters version Id3v2Version Returns int Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later. Id3v230: Where the extended header size, currently 6 or 10 bytes, excludes itself. The extended header is considered separate from the header proper, and as such is subject to unsynchronization. Id3v240 and later: Where the extended header size is the size of the whole extended header, stored as a 32 bit synchsafe integer. An extended header can thus never have a size of fewer than six bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.Id3v2F.title: Method ReadEncodingTypeFromStream | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.Id3v2F.summary: Method ReadEncodingTypeFromStream Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ReadEncodingTypeFromStream(StreamBuffer) Reads the Id3v2FrameEncodingType from the stream. public static Id3v2FrameEncodingType ReadEncodingTypeFromStream(StreamBuffer streamBuffer) Parameters streamBuffer StreamBuffer The stream buffer. Returns Id3v2FrameEncodingType The Id3v2FrameEncodingType. Remarks Since the encoding type is first read from the stream, the position should be set to the byte containing the encoding type. If the encoding type is UTF16LittleEndian, it will try to read the byte order marker from the stream to see if the encoding type is UTF16BigEndian. If no byte order marker is found or if the byte order marker is not recognized, it will return UTF16LittleEndian as default encoding. The position of the stream will not be modified.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.IsRead.summary: Property IsReadOnly Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll IsReadOnly Gets or sets a value indicating whether the frame is flagged read only. public bool IsReadOnly { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the frame is flagged as read only; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later. The read only flag, if set, tells the software that the contents of this frame is intended to be read only. Changing the contents might break something, e.g. a signature. If the contents are changed, without knowledge in why the frame was flagged read only and without taking the proper means to compensate, e.g. recalculating the signature, the flag bit will be cleared.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.TagAlt.title: Property TagAlterPreservation | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.TagAlt.summary: Property TagAlterPreservation Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TagAlterPreservation Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Id3v2Frame should be preserved after alteration of the Id3v2Tag. public bool TagAlterPreservation { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the Id3v2Frame should be preserved after alternation of the Id3v2Tag; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later. This property tells the software what to do with this Id3v2Frame if it is unknown and the Id3v2Tag is altered in any way. This applies to all kinds of alterations, including adding more padding and reordering the Frames.

Remarks When UseEncryption is set, Cryptor will be called to encrypt the data. Note that the data passed to the Cryptor function will be compressed if UseCompression is set.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.UseCom.title: Property UseCompression | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.UseCom.summary: Property UseCompression Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseCompression Gets or sets a value indicating whether the frame uses compression. public bool UseCompression { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the frame uses compression; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later. If the tag version is Id3v2.4.0, a data length indicator will be automatically added after the frame header, and the data length indicator flag will be set to true.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.UseDat.title: Property UseDataLengthIndicator | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Frame.UseDat.summary: Property UseDataLengthIndicator Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseDataLengthIndicator Gets or sets a value indicating whether the frame uses a data length indicator field. public bool UseDataLengthIndicator { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the frame uses a data length indicator field; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v240 and later. If the compression flag is set, this flag will be set to true and a data length indicator will automatically be added after the header. Setting the data length indicator flag to false when UseCompression is true will not update the property.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2FrameEncodin.title: Enum Id3v2FrameEncodingType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2FrameEncodin.summary: Enum Id3v2FrameEncodingType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Id3v2 encoding values used to specify how to encode text frames. public enum Id3v2FrameEncodingType Fields Default = 0 The default text encoding; ISO-8859-1 [ISO-8859-1]. Terminated with 0x00. UTF16BigEndian = 2 UTF-16 Big Endian [UTF-16] encoded Unicode [UNICODE] with BOM. All strings in the same frame SHALL have the same byte order. Terminated with 0x00 0x00. UTF16BigEndianWithoutBom = 3 UTF-16 Big Endian [UTF-16] encoded Unicode [UNICODE] without BOM. Terminated with 0x00 0x00. Only supported in version Id3v240 and later. UTF16LittleEndian = 1 UTF-16 Little Endian [UTF-16] encoded Unicode [UNICODE] with BOM. All strings in the same frame SHALL have the same byte order. Terminated with 0x00 0x00. UTF7 = 5 UTF-7 [UTF-7] encoded Unicode [UNICODE] with BOM. Not officially supported. Not supported in any version; included for testing purpose only. UTF8 = 4 UTF-8 [UTF-8] encoded Unicode [UNICODE]. Terminated with 0x00. Only supported in version Id3v240 and later.

everyone involved in a project. The Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame frame is a frame containing the names of those involved, and how they were involved. This frame has been replaced by the two frames MusicianCreditsList and InvolvedPeopleList2 as of Id3v240. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to but not including Id3v240. Id3v2Frame.UseDataLengthIndicator Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifier(Id3v2Version, string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidLanguageCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCountryCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCurrencyCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidUrl(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidDefaultTextString(string, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTextString(string, Id3v2FrameEncodingType, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTimeStampFormat(Id3v2TimeStampFormat) Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifierByte(short) Id3v2Frame.IsValidVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifierFieldLength(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream<T>(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks To keep space waste as low as possible this frame may be used to link information from another Id3v2 tag that might reside in another audio file or alone in a binary file. It is recommended that this method is only used when the files are stored on a CD-ROM or other circumstances when the risk of file separation is low. The frame contains a frame identifier, which is the frame that should be linked into this tag, a URL [URL] field, where a reference to the file where the frame is given, and additional ID data, if needed. Data should be retrieved from the first tag found in the file to which this link points. A linked frame is to be considered as part of the Id3v2Tag and has the same restrictions as if it was a physical part of the tag (i.e. only one Id3v2ReverbFrame frame allowed, whether it's linked or not). Frames that may be linked and need no additional data are Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame, Id3v2MusicCdIdentifierFrame, Id3v2EventTimingCodesFrame, Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame, Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustmentFrame, Id3v2EqualisationFrame, Id3v2ReverbFrame, Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame, the Id3v2TextFrames and the Id3v2UrlLinkFrame frames. The Id3v2UserDefinedTextInformationFrame, Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame, Id3v2GeneralEncapsulatedObjectFrame, Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame and Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame frames may be linked with the content descriptor as AdditionalIdData. The Id3v2CommentFrame, Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame and Id3v2UnsynchronizedLyricsFrame frames may be linked with three bytes of language descriptor directly followed by a content descriptor as AdditionalIdData. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to and including Id3v240. Constructors Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame(Id3v2Version, string) Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame class. Properties AdditionalIdData Gets or sets the additional id data. Data Gets the data and only the data of this frame. FrameIdentifier Gets or sets the frame identifier. Identifier Gets the identifier of this frame. Url Gets or sets the URL. Methods Equals(Id3v2Frame?) Equals the specified Id3v2Frame. Equals(Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame?) Equals the specified Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame. IsVersionSupported(Id3v2Version) Determines whether the specified version is supported by the frame.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2MpegLookupTa.summary: Property DeviationInBytes Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll DeviationInBytes Gets the deviation in bytes. public byte DeviationInBytes { get; } Property Value byte Remarks Each reference consists of two parts; a certain number of bits, as defined in bits for bytes deviation, that describes the difference between what is said in bytes between reference and the reality and a certain number of bits, as defined in bits for milliseconds deviation, that describes the difference between what is said in milliseconds between reference and the reality. api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2RecommendedB.summary: Property UseEmbeddedInfo Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseEmbeddedInfo Gets or sets a value indicating whether to include an Id3v2Tag in the audio stream. public bool UseEmbeddedInfo { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the audio stream should include an Id3v2Tag; otherwise, false. Remarks If the embedded info flag is true (1) then this indicates that an ID3 tag with the maximum size described in BufferSize may occur in the audio stream. In such case the tag should reside between two MPEG [MPEG] frames, if the audio is MPEG encoded. If the position of the next tag is known, OffsetToNextTag may be used.

object.ToString() Remarks Embedded tags are generally not recommended since this could render unpredictable behavior from present software/hardware. For applications like streaming audio it might be an idea to embed tags into the audio stream though. If the clients connects to individual connections like HTTP and there is a possibility to begin every transmission with a tag, then this tag should include an Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame frame. If the client is connected to a arbitrary point in the stream, such as radio or multicast, then the Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame frame SHOULD be included in every Id3v2Tag. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to and including Id3v240. played. The purpose is to be able to align all files to a reference volume, so that you don't have to change the volume constantly. This frame may also be used to balance adjust the audio. The volume adjustment is encoded as a fixed point decibel value, 16 bit signed integer representing (adjustment*512), giving +/- 64 dB with a precision of 0.001953125 dB. E.g. +2 dB is stored as 0x04 0x00 and -2 dB is 0xFC 0x00. This frame supports Id3v2Version Id3v240 only. api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.N.title: Property NameCode | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.N.summary: Property NameCode Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll NameCode Gets or sets the name code. public Id3v2NameCode NameCode { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2NameCode The name code. Remarks For each Replay Gain Adjustment field, if the name code is NotSet, then players should ignore the rest of that individual field. For each Replay Gain Adjustment field, if the name code is an unrecognized value (i.e. not RadioGainAdjustment or AudiophileGainAdjustment), then players should ignore the rest of that individual field. If no valid Replay Gain Adjustment fields are found (i.e. all name codes are either NotSet or unknown), then the player should proceed as if the file contained no Replay Gain Adjustment information.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.N.title: Field NameCodeMask | AudioVideoLib

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.O.title: Property OriginatorCode | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.O.summary: Property OriginatorCode Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginatorCode Gets or sets the originator code. public Id3v2OriginatorCode OriginatorCode { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2OriginatorCode The originator code. Remarks For each Replay Gain Adjustment field, if the name code is valid, but the Originator code is Unspecified, then the player should ignore that Replay Gain adjustment field. For each Replay Gain Adjustment field, if the name code is valid, but the Originator code is unknown, then the player should still use the information within that Replay Gain Adjustment field. This is because, even if we are unsure as to how the adjustment was determined, any valid Replay Gain adjustment is more useful than none at all. If no valid Replay Gain Adjustment fields are found (i.e. all originator codes are Unspecified), then the player should proceed as if the file contained no Replay Gain Adjustment information.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.h.title: Class Id3v2ReplayGain | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReplayGain.h.summary: Class Id3v2ReplayGain Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Class for storing ReplayGain. public sealed class Id3v2ReplayGain Inheritance object Id3v2ReplayGain Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks ReplayGain is the name of a technique invented to achieve the same perceived playback loudness of audio files. It defines an algorithm to measure the perceived loudness of audio data. ReplayGain allows the loudness of each song within a collection of songs to be consistent. This is called 'Track Gain' (or 'Radio Gain' in earlier parlance). It also allows the loudness of a specific sub-collection (an "album") to be consistent with the rest of the collection, while allowing the dynamics from song to song on the album to remain intact. This is called 'Album Gain' (or 'Audiophile Gain' in earlier parlance). This is especially important when listening to classical music albums, because quiet tracks need to remain a certain degree quieter than the louder ones. ReplayGain is different from peak normalization. Peak normalization merely ensures that the peak amplitude reaches a certain level. This does not ensure equal loudness. The ReplayGain technique measures the effective power of the waveform (i.e. the RMS power after applying an "equal loudness contour"), and then adjusts the amplitude of the waveform accordingly. The result is that Replay Gained waveforms are usually more uniformly amplified than peak-normalized waveforms. Fields AdjustmentMask The adjustment mask. NameCodeMask The name code mask. OriginatorCodeMask The originator code mask. SignMask The sign mask. Properties Adjustment Gets or sets the replay gain adjustment. NameCode Gets or sets the name code. OriginatorCode Gets or sets the originator code. Sign Gets or sets the replay gain adjustment sign. Methods ToByteArray() Writes the Id3v2ReplayGain into a byte array.

Players can scale the audio data by this replay gain in order to achieve a consistent perceived loudness across all tracks. The reference gain is 83dB SPL, as defined in the SMPTE RP 200 standard. This frame supports Id3v2Version Id3v230 and later. Constructors Id3v2ReplayGainAdjustmentFrame() Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2ReplayGainAdjustmentFrame class with version Id3v230. api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReverbFrame..title: Property PremixLeftToRight | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReverbFrame..summary: Property PremixLeftToRight Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PremixLeftToRight Gets or sets the premix left to right. public byte PremixLeftToRight { get; set; } Property Value byte The premix left to right. Remarks 'Premix left to right' is the amount of left sound to be mixed in the right before any reverb is applied, where 0x00 id 0% and 0xFF is 100%. 'Premix right to left' does the same thing, but right to left. Setting both premix to 0xFF would result in a mono output (if the reverb is applied symmetric).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReverbFrame..summary: Property ReverbBouncesRight Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ReverbBouncesRight Gets or sets the reverb bounces right. public byte ReverbBouncesRight { get; set; } Property Value byte The reverb bounces right. api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReverbFrame..title: Property ReverbFeedbackLeftToLeft | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2ReverbFrame..summary: Property ReverbFeedbackLeftToLeft Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ReverbFeedbackLeftToLeft Gets or sets the reverb feedback left to left. public byte ReverbFeedbackLeftToLeft { get; set; } Property Value byte The reverb feedback left to left. Remarks Feedback is the amount of volume that should be returned to the next echo bounce. 0x00 is 0%, 0xFF is 100%. If this value were 0x7F, there would be 50% volume reduction on the first bounce, yet 50% on the second and so on. Left to left means the sound from the left bounce to be played in the left speaker, while left to right means sound from the left bounce to be played in the right speaker.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AlbumSor.title: Property AlbumSortOrder | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AlbumSor.summary: Property AlbumSortOrder Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AlbumSortOrder Gets or sets the album sort order. public Id3v2TextFrame? AlbumSortOrder { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The album sort order. Remarks The 'Album sort order' frame defines a string which should be used instead of the album name (TALB) for sorting purposes. E.g. an album named "A Soundtrack" might preferably be sorted as "Soundtrack". This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

Remarks The 'Album/Movie/Show title' frame is intended for the title of the recording (or source of sound) from which the audio in the file is taken.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Artist.h.title: Property Artist | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Artist.h.summary: Property Artist Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Artist Gets or sets the artist. public Id3v2TextFrame? Artist { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The artists. Remarks The 'Lead artist(s)/Lead performer(s)/Soloist(s)/Performing group' is used for the main artist(s).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ArtistEx.title: Property ArtistExtra | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ArtistEx.summary: Property ArtistExtra Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ArtistExtra Gets or sets the artist extra. public Id3v2TextFrame? ArtistExtra { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The artists extra. Remarks The 'Band/Orchestra/Accompaniment' frame is used for additional information about the performers in the recording.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Attached.title: Property AttachedPictures | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Attached.summary: Property AttachedPictures Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AttachedPictures Gets or sets a list of attached pictures. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame> AttachedPictures { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame> The attached pictures. Remarks There may only be one picture with the picture type declared as FileIcon and OtherFileIcon respectively. There may be several Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame in one file, each in their individual Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame, but only one with the same Description.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioEnc.title: Property AudioEncryptions | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioEnc.summary: Property AudioEncryptions Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AudioEncryptions Gets or sets a list of audio encryptions. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame> AudioEncryptions { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame> A list of audio encryptions. Remarks This property indicates if the actual audio stream is encrypted, and by whom. Since standardization of such encryption scheme is beyond this document, all Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame frames contain an URL which can be an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted audio file. Questions regarding the encrypted audio should be sent to the email address specified. There may be more than one Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame frames in an Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same OwnerIdentifier.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioSee.title: Property AudioSeekPointIndex | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioSee.summary: Property AudioSeekPointIndex Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AudioSeekPointIndex Gets or sets the audio seek point index. public Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame? AudioSeekPointIndex { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame The audio seek point index. Remarks Audio files with variable bitrates are intrinsically difficult to deal with in the case of seeking within the file. The Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame frame makes seeking easier by providing a list a seek points within the audio file. The seek points are a fractional offset within the audio data, providing a starting point from which to find an appropriate point to start decoding. The presence of an Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame frame requires the existence of the Length property, indicating the duration of the file in milliseconds. There may only be one Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioSiz.title: Property AudioSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.AudioSiz.summary: Property AudioSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AudioSize Gets or sets the size of the audio. public Id3v2TextFrame? AudioSize { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The sizes of the audio. Remarks The 'Size' frame contains the size of the audio file in bytes excluding the tag, represented as a numeric string. This frame has been removed as of Id3v240. The information contained in this frame is in the general case either trivial to calculate for the player or impossible for the tagger to calculate. There is however no good use for such information. The frame is therefore completely deprecated.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.BeatsPer.title: Property BeatsPerMinute | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.BeatsPer.summary: Property BeatsPerMinute Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll BeatsPerMinute Gets or sets the beats per minute. public Id3v2TextFrame? BeatsPerMinute { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The beats per minute. Remarks BPM is short for beats per minute, and is easily computed by dividing the number of beats in a musical piece with its length. To get a more accurate result, do the BPM calculation on the main-part only. To acquire best result measure the time between each beat and calculate individual BPM for each beat and use the median value as result. BPM is an integer and represented as a numerical string.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Calculat.title: Method CalculateCrc32 | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Calculat.summary: Method CalculateCrc32 Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll CalculateCrc32() Calculates the CRC32 of the Id3v2Tag. public int CalculateCrc32() Returns int The CRC32 of the Id3v2Tag. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Comments.title: Property Comments | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Comments.summary: Property Comments Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Comments Gets or sets a list of comments. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2CommentFrame> Comments { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2CommentFrame> The comments. Remarks There may be more than one Id3v2CommentFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Language and ShortContentDescription.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Commerci.title: Property Commercial | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Commerci.summary: Property Commercial Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Commercial Gets or sets the commercial frame. public Id3v2CommercialFrame? Commercial { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2CommercialFrame The commercial frame. Remarks This frame enables several competing offers in the same tag by bundling all needed information. That makes this frame rather complex but it's an easier solution than if one tries to achieve the same result with several frames. There may be more than one Id3v2CommercialFrame in an Id3v2Tag, but no two may be identical. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Commerci.title: Property CommercialInformations | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Commerci.summary: Property CommercialInformations Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll CommercialInformations Gets or sets a collection of commercial information. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UrlLinkFrame> CommercialInformations { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UrlLinkFrame> A collection commercial information. Remarks The 'Commercial information' frame is a URL pointing at a webpage with information such as where the album can be bought. There may be more than one "WCOM" frame in a tag, but not with the same content.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Composer.title: Property ComposerName | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Composer.summary: Property ComposerName Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ComposerName Gets or sets the name of the composer. public Id3v2TextFrame? ComposerName { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The name of the composer. Remarks The 'Composer(s)' frame is intended for the name of the composer(s).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Compress.title: Property CompressedDataMeta | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Compress.summary: Property CompressedDataMeta Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll CompressedDataMeta Gets or sets the audio seek point index. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2CompressedDataMetaFrame> CompressedDataMeta { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2CompressedDataMetaFrame> The audio seek point index. Remarks This frame is specific to Id3v221.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Conducto.title: Property ConductorName | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Conducto.summary: Property ConductorName Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ConductorName Gets or sets the name of the conductor. public Id3v2TextFrame? ConductorName { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The name of the conductor. Remarks The 'Conductor' frame is used for the name of the conductor.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ContentG.title: Property ContentGroupDescription | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ContentG.summary: Property ContentGroupDescription Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ContentGroupDescription Gets or sets the content group description. public Id3v2TextFrame? ContentGroupDescription { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The group description. Remarks The 'Content group description' frame is used if the sound belongs to a larger category of sounds/music. For example, classical music is often sorted in different musical sections (e.g. "Piano Concerto", "Weather - Hurricane").

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ContentT.title: Property ContentType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ContentT.summary: Property ContentType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ContentType Gets or sets the type of the content. public Id3v2TextFrame? ContentType { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The type of the content. Remarks The content type, which previously (in Id3v1.1, see appendix A) was stored as a one byte numeric value only, is now a numeric string. You may use one or several of the types as Id3v1.1 did or, since the category list would be impossible to maintain with accurate and up to date categories, define your own. References to the Id3v1 genres can be made by, as first byte, enter "(" followed by a number from the genres list (section A.3.) and ended with a ")" character. This is optionally followed by a refinement, e.g. "(21)" or "(4)Eurodisco". Several references can be made in the same frame, e.g. "(51)(39)". If the refinement should begin with a "(" character it should be replaced with "((", e.g. "((I can figure out any genre)" or "(55)((I think...)". The following new content types is defined in Id3v2 and is implemented in the same way as the numeric content types, e.g. "(RX)". RX Remix CR Cover

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Copyrigh.title: Property CopyrightInformation | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Copyrigh.summary: Property CopyrightInformation Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll CopyrightInformation Gets or sets the copyright information. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? CopyrightInformation { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The copyright information. Remarks The 'Copyright/Legal information' frame is a URL pointing at a webpage where the terms of use and ownership of the file is described.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Copyrigh.title: Property CopyrightMessage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Copyrigh.summary: Property CopyrightMessage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll CopyrightMessage Gets or sets the copyright message. public Id3v2TextFrame? CopyrightMessage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The copyright message. Remarks The 'Copyright message' frame, which must begin with a year and a space character (making five characters), is intended for the copyright holder of the original sound, not the audio file itself. The absence of this frame means only that the copyright information is unavailable or has been removed, and must not be interpreted to mean that the sound is public domain. Every time this field is displayed the field must be preceded with "Copyright " (C) " ", where (C) is one character showing a C in a circle.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.DateReco.title: Property DateRecording | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.DateReco.summary: Property DateRecording Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll DateRecording Gets or sets the date for the recording. public Id3v2TextFrame? DateRecording { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The date for the recording. Remarks The 'Date' frame is a numeric string in the DDMM format containing the date for the recording. This field is always four characters long. This frame has been replaced by the TDRC frame, 'Recording time' as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.EncodedB.title: Property EncodedBy | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.EncodedB.summary: Property EncodedBy Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncodedBy Gets or sets the encoded by. public Id3v2TextFrame? EncodedBy { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The encoded by. Remarks The 'Encoded by' frame contains the name of the person or organization that encoded the audio file. This field may contain a copyright message, if the audio file also is copyrighted by the encoder.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encoding.title: Property EncodingSettingsUsed | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encoding.summary: Property EncodingSettingsUsed Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncodingSettingsUsed Gets or sets the encoding settings used. public Id3v2TextFrame? EncodingSettingsUsed { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The encoding settings used. Remarks The 'Software/hardware and settings used for encoding' frame includes the used audio encoder and its settings when the file was encoded. Hardware refers to hardware encoders, not the computer on which a program was run.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encoding.title: Property EncodingTime | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encoding.summary: Property EncodingTime Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncodingTime Gets or sets the encoding time. public Id3v2TextFrame? EncodingTime { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The encoding time. Remarks The 'Encoding time' frame contains a timestamp describing when the audio was encoded. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encrypte.title: Property EncryptedMetaFrames | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encrypte.summary: Property EncryptedMetaFrames Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncryptedMetaFrames Gets or sets a list of encrypted meta frames. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame> EncryptedMetaFrames { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame> A list of encrypted meta frames. Remarks This property contains one or more encrypted frames. This enables protection of copyrighted information such as pictures and text, that people might want to pay extra for. Since standardization of such an encryption scheme is beyond this document, all Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame frames contain an URL [URL] containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific encrypted meta frame. Questions regarding the encrypted frame should be sent to the indicated email address. When an Id3v2 decoder encounters an Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame frame, it should send the data block to the 'plugin' with the corresponding OwnerIdentifier and expect to receive either a data block with one or several Id3v2 frames after each other or an error. There may be more than one Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same OwnerIdentifier. This frame has been removed as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encrypti.title: Property EncryptionMethodRegistrations | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Encrypti.summary: Property EncryptionMethodRegistrations Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll EncryptionMethodRegistrations Gets or sets a list of encryption method registrations. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2EncryptionMethodRegistrationFrame> EncryptionMethodRegistrations { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2EncryptionMethodRegistrationFrame> A list of encryption method registrations. Remarks To identify with which method a frame has been encrypted the encryption method must be registered in the tag with this frame. There may be several Id3v2EncryptionMethodRegistrationFrame frames in an Id3v2Tag but only one containing the same MethodSymbol and only one containing the same OwnerIdentifier. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Equalisa.title: Property Equalisation | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Equalisa.summary: Property Equalisation Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Equalisation Gets or sets the equalisation. public Id3v2EqualisationFrame? Equalisation { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2EqualisationFrame The equalisation. Remarks This field allows the user to predefine an equalisation curve within the audio file. There may only be one Id3v2EqualisationFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag. This frame has been replaced by the Id3v2Equalisation2Frame frame as of Id3v240.

Remarks This frame allows the user to predefine an equalisation curve within the audio file. There may be more than one Id3v2Equalisation2Frame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the Identification. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Experime.summary: Property ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2 Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2 Gets or sets a list of experimental relative volume adjustment (2). public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame> ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2 { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame> A list of experimental relative volume adjustment (2). There may be more than one Id3v2ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Identification. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.FileOwne.title: Property FileOwner | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.FileOwne.summary: Property FileOwner Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FileOwner Gets or sets the file owner. public Id3v2TextFrame? FileOwner { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The owner of the file. Remarks The 'File owner/licensee' frame contains the name of the owner or licensee of the file and it's contents. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.FileType.title: Property FileType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.FileType.summary: Property FileType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FileType Gets or sets the type of the file. public Id3v2TextFrame? FileType { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The type of the file. Remarks The 'File type' frame indicates which type of audio this tag defines. The following type and refinements are defined: MPG MPEG Audio /1 MPEG 2 layer I /2 MPEG 2 layer II /3 MPEG 2 layer III /2.5 MPEG 2.5 /AAC Advanced audio compression but other types may be used, not for these types though. This is used in a similar way to the predefined types in the "TMT" frame, but without parenthesis. If this frame is not present audio type is assumed to be "MPG".

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.GroupIde.summary: Property GroupIdentificationRegistrations Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll GroupIdentificationRegistrations Gets or sets a list of group identification registrations. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2GroupIdentificationRegistrationFrame> GroupIdentificationRegistrations { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2GroupIdentificationRegistrationFrame> A list of group identification registrations. Remarks This frame enables grouping of otherwise unrelated frames. This can be used when some frames are to be signed. To identify which frames belongs to a set of frames a group identifier must be registered in the tag with this frame. There may be several Id3v2GroupIdentificationRegistrationFrame frames in an Id3v2Tag but only one containing the same GroupSymbol and only one containing the same OwnerIdentifier. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.HeaderId.summary: Field HeaderIdentifier Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The header identifier for an Id3v2Tag.
