The first three bytes of the tag are always "ID3" to indicate that this is an ID3 tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.HeaderSi.title: Field HeaderSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.HeaderSi.summary: Field HeaderSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The size of the Id3v2Tag header, in bytes. public const int HeaderSize = 10 Returns int The size of the header, in bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.InitialK.title: Property InitialKey | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.InitialK.summary: Property InitialKey Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InitialKey Gets or sets the initial key. public Id3v2TextFrame? InitialKey { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The initial key. Remarks The 'Initial key' frame contains the musical key in which the sound starts. It is represented as a string with a maximum length of three characters. The ground keys are represented with "A","B","C","D","E", "F" and "G" and half keys represented with "b" and "#". Minor is represented as "m". Example "Cbm". Off key is represented with an "o" only.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internat.title: Property InternationalStandardRecordingCode | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internat.summary: Property InternationalStandardRecordingCode Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InternationalStandardRecordingCode Gets or sets the international standard recording code. public Id3v2TextFrame? InternationalStandardRecordingCode { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The international standard recording code. Remarks The 'ISRC' frame should contain the International Standard Recording Code [ISRC] (12 characters).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internet.title: Property InternetRadioStationName | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internet.summary: Property InternetRadioStationName Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InternetRadioStationName Gets or sets the name of the internet radio station. public Id3v2TextFrame? InternetRadioStationName { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The name of the internet radio station. Remarks The 'Internet radio station name' frame contains the name of the internet radio station from which the audio is streamed. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internet.title: Property InternetRadioStationOwner | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Internet.summary: Property InternetRadioStationOwner Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InternetRadioStationOwner Gets or sets the internet radio station owner. public Id3v2TextFrame? InternetRadioStationOwner { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The internet radio station owner. Remarks The 'Internet radio station owner' frame contains the name of the owner of the internet radio station from which the audio is streamed. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Involved.title: Property InvolvedPeopleList | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Involved.summary: Property InvolvedPeopleList Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InvolvedPeopleList Gets or sets a list of involved people. public Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame? InvolvedPeopleList { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame The involved people list. Remarks Since there might be a lot of people contributing to an audio file in various ways, such as musicians and technicians, the 'Text information frames' are often insufficient to list everyone involved in a project. The 'Involved people list' is a frame containing the names of those involved, and how they were involved. There may only be one Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag. This frame has been replaced by the two frames MusicianCreditsList and InvolvedPeopleList2 as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Involved.title: Property InvolvedPeopleList2 | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Involved.summary: Property InvolvedPeopleList2 Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll InvolvedPeopleList2 Gets or sets the involved people list. public Id3v2TextFrame? InvolvedPeopleList2 { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The involved people list. Remarks 'Involved people list' is very similar to the musician credits list, but maps between functions, like producer, and names. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Length.h.title: Property Length | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Length.h.summary: Property Length Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Length Gets or sets the length. public Id3v2TextFrame? Length { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The length. Remarks The 'Length' frame contains the length of the audio file in milliseconds, represented as a numeric string.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.LinkedIn.title: Property LinkedInformations | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.LinkedIn.summary: Property LinkedInformations Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll LinkedInformations Gets or sets a list of linked information. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame> LinkedInformations { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame> A list of linked information. Remarks To keep space waste as low as possible this property may be used to link information from another Id3v2 tag that might reside in another audio file or alone in a binary file. It is recommended that this method is only used when the files are stored on a CD-ROM or other circumstances when the risk of file separation is low. The frame contains a frame identifier, which is the frame that should be linked into this tag, a URL [URL] field, where a reference to the file where the frame is given, and additional ID data, if needed. Data should be retrieved from the first tag found in the file to which this link points. There may be more than one Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same FrameIdentifier, Url and AdditionalIdData. A linked frame is to be considered as part of the Id3v2Tag and has the same restrictions as if it was a physical part of the tag (i.e. only one Id3v2ReverbFrame frame allowed, whether it's linked or not). Frames that may be linked and need no additional data are Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame, Id3v2MusicCdIdentifierFrame, Id3v2EventTimingCodesFrame, Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame, Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustmentFrame, Id3v2EqualisationFrame, Id3v2ReverbFrame, Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame, the Id3v2TextFrames and the Id3v2UrlLinkFrame frames. The Id3v2UserDefinedTextInformationFrame, Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame, Id3v2GeneralEncapsulatedObjectFrame, Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame and Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame frames may be linked with the content descriptor as AdditionalIdData. The Id3v2CommentFrame, Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame and Id3v2UnsynchronizedLyricsFrame frames may be linked with three bytes of language descriptor directly followed by a content descriptor as AdditionalIdData.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MaxAllow.title: Field MaxAllowedFrames | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MaxAllow.summary: Field MaxAllowedFrames Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The max amounts of Id3v2Frames an Id3v2Tag can have. public const int MaxAllowedFrames = 1048575 Returns int The max amounts of s an can have.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MaxAllow.title: Field MaxAllowedSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MaxAllow.summary: Field MaxAllowedSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The max size the Id3v2Tag can be. public const int MaxAllowedSize = 268435455 Returns int The max size the can be. Remarks The max size of an Id3v2Tag is 256MB.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MediaTyp.title: Property MediaType | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MediaTyp.summary: Property MediaType Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MediaType Gets or sets the type of the media. public Id3v2TextFrame? MediaType { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The type of the media. Remarks The 'Media type' frame describes from which media the sound originated. This may be a text string or a reference to the predefined media types found in the list below. References are made within "(" and ")" and are optionally followed by a text refinement, e.g. "(MC) with four channels". If a text refinement should begin with a "(" character it should be replaced with "((" in the same way as in the "TCO" frame. Predefined refinements is appended after the media type, e.g. "(CD/S)" or "(VID/PAL/VHS)". DIG Other digital media /A Analog transfer from media ANA Other analog media /WAC Wax cylinder /8CA 8-track tape cassette CD CD /A Analog transfer from media /DD DDD /AD ADD /AA AAD LD Laserdisc /A Analog transfer from media TT Turntable records /33 33.33 rpm /45 45 rpm /71 71.29 rpm /76 76.59 rpm /78 78.26 rpm /80 80 rpm MD MiniDisc /A Analog transfer from media DAT DAT /A Analog transfer from media /1 standard, 48 kHz/16 bits, linear /2 mode 2, 32 kHz/16 bits, linear /3 mode 3, 32 kHz/12 bits, nonlinear, low speed /4 mode 4, 32 kHz/12 bits, 4 channels /5 mode 5, 44.1 kHz/16 bits, linear /6 mode 6, 44.1 kHz/16 bits, 'wide track' play DCC DCC /A Analog transfer from media DVD DVD /A Analog transfer from media TV Television /PAL PAL /NTSC NTSC /SECAM SECAM VID Video /PAL PAL /NTSC NTSC /SECAM SECAM /VHS VHS /SVHS S-VHS /BETA BETAMAX RAD Radio /FM FM /AM AM /LW LW /MW MW TEL Telephone /I ISDN MC MC (normal cassette) /4 4.75 cm/s (normal speed for a two sided cassette) /9 9.5 cm/s /I Type I cassette (ferric/normal) /II Type II cassette (chrome) /III Type III cassette (ferric chrome) /IV Type IV cassette (metal) REE Reel /9 9.5 cm/s /19 19 cm/s /38 38 cm/s /76 76 cm/s /I Type I cassette (ferric/normal) /II Type II cassette (chrome) /III Type III cassette (ferric chrome) /IV Type IV cassette (metal)

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Modified.title: Property ModifiedBy | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Modified.summary: Property ModifiedBy Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ModifiedBy Gets or sets the modified by. public Id3v2TextFrame? ModifiedBy { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The modified by. Remarks The 'Interpreted, remixed, or otherwise modified by' frame contains more information about the people behind a remix and similar interpretations of another existing piece.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Mood.htm.title: Property Mood | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Mood.htm.summary: Property Mood Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Mood Gets or sets the mood. public Id3v2TextFrame? Mood { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The mood of the audio. Remarks The 'Mood' frame is intended to reflect the mood of the audio with a few keywords, e.g. "Romantic" or "Sad". This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MpegLoca.title: Property MpegLocationLookupTable | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MpegLoca.summary: Property MpegLocationLookupTable Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MpegLocationLookupTable Gets or sets the MPEG location lookup table. public Id3v2MpegLocationLookupTableFrame? MpegLocationLookupTable { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2MpegLocationLookupTableFrame The MPEG location lookup table. Remarks This field includes references that the software can use to calculate positions in the file. There may only be one Id3v2MpegLocationLookupTableFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MusicCdI.title: Property MusicCdIdentifier | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.MusicCdI.summary: Property MusicCdIdentifier Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MusicCdIdentifier Gets or sets the music CD identifier. public Id3v2MusicCdIdentifierFrame? MusicCdIdentifier { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2MusicCdIdentifierFrame The music CD identifier. Remarks This frame is intended for music that comes from a CD, so that the CD can be identified in databases such as the CDDB [CDDB]. The frame consists of a binary dump of the Table Of Contents, TOC, from the CD, which is a header of 4 bytes and then 8 bytes/track on the CD making a maximum of 804 bytes. This frame requires a present and valid TrackNumber. There may only be one Id3v2MusicCdIdentifierFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Musician.title: Property MusicianCreditsList | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Musician.summary: Property MusicianCreditsList Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll MusicianCreditsList Gets or sets the musician credits list. public Id3v2TextFrame? MusicianCreditsList { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The musician credits list. Remarks The 'Musician credits list' is intended as a mapping between instruments and the musician that played it. Every odd field is an instrument and every even is an artist or a comma delimited list of artists. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.title: Property OfficialArtistWebpage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.summary: Property OfficialArtistWebpage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OfficialArtistWebpage Gets or sets the official artist webpage. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UrlLinkFrame> OfficialArtistWebpage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UrlLinkFrame> The official artist webpage. Remarks The 'Official artist/performer webpage' frame is a URL pointing at the artists official webpage. There may be more than one "WOAR" frame in a tag if the audio contains more than one performer, but not with the same content.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.title: Property OfficialAudioFileWebpage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.summary: Property OfficialAudioFileWebpage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OfficialAudioFileWebpage Gets or sets the official audio file webpage. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? OfficialAudioFileWebpage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The official audio file webpage. Remarks The 'Official audio file webpage' frame is a URL pointing at a file specific webpage.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.title: Property OfficialAudioSource | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.summary: Property OfficialAudioSource Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OfficialAudioSource Gets or sets the official audio source. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? OfficialAudioSource { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The official audio source. Remarks The 'Official audio source webpage' frame is a URL pointing at the official webpage for the source of the audio file, e.g. a movie.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.title: Property OfficialInternetRadioStationHomepage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Official.summary: Property OfficialInternetRadioStationHomepage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OfficialInternetRadioStationHomepage Gets or sets the official internet radio station homepage. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? OfficialInternetRadioStationHomepage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The official internet radio station homepage. Remarks The 'Official internet radio station homepage' contains a URL pointing at the homepage of the internet radio station.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalAlbumTitle | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalAlbumTitle Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalAlbumTitle Gets or sets the original album title. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalAlbumTitle { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original album title. Remarks The 'Original album/Movie/Show title' frame is intended for the title of the original recording(/source of sound), if for example the music in the file should be a cover of a previously released song.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalArtist | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalArtist Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalArtist Gets or sets the original artist. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalArtist { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original artist. Remarks The 'Original artist(s)/performer(s)' frame is intended for the performer(s) of the original recording, if for example the music in the file should be a cover of a previously released song.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalFilename | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalFilename Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalFilename Gets or sets the original filename. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalFilename { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original filename. Remarks The 'Original filename' frame contains the preferred filename for the file, since some media doesn't allow the desired length of the filename. The filename is case sensitive and includes its suffix.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalReleaseTime | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalReleaseTime Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalReleaseTime Gets or sets the original release time. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalReleaseTime { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original release time. Remarks The 'Original release time' frame contains a timestamp describing when the original recording of the audio was released. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalReleaseYear | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalReleaseYear Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalReleaseYear Gets or sets the original release year. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalReleaseYear { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original release year. Remarks The 'Original release year' frame is intended for the year when the original recording, if for example the music in the file should be a cover of a previously released song, was released. The field is formatted as in the "TDY" frame. This frame has been replaced by the TDOR frame, 'Original release time' as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.title: Property OriginalTextWriter | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Original.summary: Property OriginalTextWriter Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OriginalTextWriter Gets or sets the original text writer. public Id3v2TextFrame? OriginalTextWriter { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The original text writer. Remarks The 'Original Lyricist(s)/text writer(s)' frame is intended for the text writer(s) of the original recording, if for example the music in the file should be a cover of a previously released song.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Ownershi.title: Property Ownership | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Ownershi.summary: Property Ownership Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Ownership Gets or sets the ownership. public Id3v2OwnershipFrame? Ownership { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2OwnershipFrame The ownership. Remarks The ownership frame might be used as a reminder of a made transaction or, if signed, as proof. Note that the Id3v2TermsOfUseFrame frame and FileOwner property are good to use in conjunction with this one. There may only be one Id3v2OwnershipFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PaddingS.title: Property PaddingSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PaddingS.summary: Property PaddingSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PaddingSize Gets or sets the amount of bytes to use as padding. public int PaddingSize { get; set; } Property Value int Remarks The 'Size of padding' is simply the total tag size excluding the frames and the headers, in other words the padding. This does not include the PaddingSize.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PartOfSe.title: Property PartOfSet | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PartOfSe.summary: Property PartOfSet Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PartOfSet Gets or sets the part of set. public Id3v2TextFrame? PartOfSet { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The part of set. Remarks The 'Part of a set' frame is a numeric string that describes which part of a set the audio came from. This frame is used if the source described in the "TAL" frame is divided into several mediums, e.g. a double CD. The value may be extended with a "/" character and a numeric string containing the total number of parts in the set. E.g. "1/2".

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PaymentW.title: Property PaymentWebpage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PaymentW.summary: Property PaymentWebpage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PaymentWebpage Gets or sets the payment webpage. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? PaymentWebpage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The payment webpage. Remarks The 'Payment' frame is a URL pointing at a webpage that will handle the process of paying for this file.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Performe.title: Property PerformerSortOrder | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Performe.summary: Property PerformerSortOrder Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PerformerSortOrder Gets or sets the performer sort order. public Id3v2TextFrame? PerformerSortOrder { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The performer sort order. Remarks The 'Performer sort order' frame defines a string which should be used instead of the performer (TPE2) for sorting purposes. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PlayCoun.title: Property PlayCounter | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PlayCoun.summary: Property PlayCounter Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PlayCounter Gets or sets the play counter. public Id3v2PlayCounterFrame? PlayCounter { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2PlayCounterFrame Remarks This is simply a counter of the number of times a file has been played. There may only be one Id3v2PlayCounterFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Playlist.title: Property PlaylistDelay | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Playlist.summary: Property PlaylistDelay Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PlaylistDelay Gets or sets the playlist delay. public Id3v2TextFrame? PlaylistDelay { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The playlist delay. Remarks The 'Playlist delay' defines the numbers of milliseconds of silence between every song in a playlist. The player should use the "ETC" frame, if present, to skip initial silence and silence at the end of the audio to match the 'Playlist delay' time. The time is represented as a numeric string.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Populari.title: Property Popularimeters | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Populari.summary: Property Popularimeters Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Popularimeters Gets or sets a list of popularimeters in the tag. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2PopularimeterFrame> Popularimeters { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2PopularimeterFrame> A list of popularimeters. Remarks The purpose of this field is to specify how good an audio file is. Many interesting applications could be found to this frame such as a playlist that features better audio files more often than others or it could be used to profile a person's taste and find other 'good' files by comparing people's profiles. There may be more than one Id3v2PopularimeterFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same EmailToUser.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Position.title: Property PositionSynchronization | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Position.summary: Property PositionSynchronization Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PositionSynchronization Gets or sets the position synchronization. public Id3v2PositionSynchronizationFrame? PositionSynchronization { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2PositionSynchronizationFrame The position synchronization. Remarks This frame delivers information to the listener of how far into the audio stream he picked up; in effect, it states the time offset of the first frame in the stream. The position is where in the audio the listener starts to receive, i.e. the beginning of the next frame. If this frame is used in the beginning of a file the value is always 0. There may only be one Id3v2PositionSynchronizationFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Preserve.title: Property PreserveFramesAfterFileAlteration | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Preserve.summary: Property PreserveFramesAfterFileAlteration Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PreserveFramesAfterFileAlteration Gets or sets a value indicating whether to preserve frames after file alteration. public bool PreserveFramesAfterFileAlteration { get; set; } Property Value bool true to preserve frames after file alteration; otherwise, false. Remarks When set to true, all frames will be written to the byte array when calling ToByteArray, regardless of the frame's FileAlterPreservation flag. Default is true.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Preserve.title: Property PreserveFramesAfterTagAlteration | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Preserve.summary: Property PreserveFramesAfterTagAlteration Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PreserveFramesAfterTagAlteration Gets or sets a value indicating whether to preserve frames after tag alteration. public bool PreserveFramesAfterTagAlteration { get; set; } Property Value bool true to preserve frames after tag alteration; otherwise, false. Remarks When set to true, all frames will be written to the byte array when calling ToByteArray, regardless of the frame's TagAlterPreservation flag. Default is true.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PrivateF.title: Property PrivateFrames | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.PrivateF.summary: Property PrivateFrames Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PrivateFrames Gets or sets a list of private frames. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2PrivateFrame> PrivateFrames { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2PrivateFrame> A list of private frames. Remarks This frame is used to contain information from a software producer that its program uses and does not fit into the other frames. The tag may contain more than one Id3v2PrivateFrame frame but only with different PrivateData. It is recommended to keep the number of Id3v2PrivateFrame frames as low as possible. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Produced.title: Property ProducedNote | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Produced.summary: Property ProducedNote Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ProducedNote Gets or sets the produced note. public Id3v2TextFrame? ProducedNote { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The produced note. Remarks The 'Produced notice' frame, in which the string must begin with a year and a space character (making five characters), is intended for the production copyright holder of the original sound, not the audio file itself. The absence of this frame means only that the production copyright information is unavailable or has been removed, and must not be interpreted to mean that the audio is public domain. Every time this field is displayed the field must be preceded with "Produced " (P) " ", where (P) is one character showing a P in a circle. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Publishe.title: Property Publisher | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Publishe.summary: Property Publisher Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Publisher Gets or sets the publisher. public Id3v2TextFrame? Publisher { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The publisher. Remarks The 'Publisher' frame simply contains the name of the label or publisher.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Publishe.title: Property PublishersOfficialWebpage | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Publishe.summary: Property PublishersOfficialWebpage Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll PublishersOfficialWebpage Gets or sets the publishers official webpage. public Id3v2UrlLinkFrame? PublishersOfficialWebpage { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2UrlLinkFrame The publishers official webpage. Remarks The 'Publishers official webpage' frame is a URL pointing at the official webpage for the publisher.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recommen.title: Property RecommendedBufferSize | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recommen.summary: Property RecommendedBufferSize Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RecommendedBufferSize Gets or sets the size of the recommended buffer. public Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame? RecommendedBufferSize { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame The size of the recommended buffer. Remarks Embedded tags are generally not recommended since this could render unpredictable behavior from present software/hardware. For applications like streaming audio it might be an idea to embed tags into the audio stream though. If the clients connects to individual connections like HTTP and there is a possibility to begin every transmission with a tag, then this tag should include an Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame frame. If the client is connected to a arbitrary point in the stream, such as radio or multicast, then the Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame frame SHOULD be included in every Id3v2Tag. There may only be one Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recordin.title: Property RecordingDates | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recordin.summary: Property RecordingDates Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RecordingDates Gets or sets the recording dates. public Id3v2TextFrame? RecordingDates { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The recording dates. Remarks The 'Recording dates' frame is a intended to be used as complement to the "TYE", "TDA" and "TIM" frames. E.g. "4th-7th June, 12th June" in combination with the "TYE" frame. This frame has been replaced by the TDRC frame, 'Recording time' as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recordin.title: Property RecordingTime | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Recordin.summary: Property RecordingTime Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RecordingTime Gets or sets the recording time. public Id3v2TextFrame? RecordingTime { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The recording time. Remarks The 'Recording time' frame contains a timestamp describing when the audio was recorded. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Relative.title: Property RelativeVolumeAdjustment | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Relative.summary: Property RelativeVolumeAdjustment Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RelativeVolumeAdjustment Gets or sets the relative volume adjustment. public Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustmentFrame? RelativeVolumeAdjustment { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustmentFrame The relative volume adjustment. Remarks This field allows the user to say how much he wants to increase/decrease the volume on each channel while the file is played. The purpose is to be able to align all files to a reference volume, so that you don't have to change the volume constantly. This frame may also be used to balance adjust the audio. If the volume peak levels are known then this could be described with the PeakVolumeRightChannel and PeakVolumeLeftChannel fields. There may only be one Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustmentFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag. This frame has been replaced by the Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame frame as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Relative.title: Property RelativeVolumeAdjustment2 | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Relative.summary: Property RelativeVolumeAdjustment2 Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RelativeVolumeAdjustment2 Gets or sets a list of relative volume adjustment (2). public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame> RelativeVolumeAdjustment2 { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame> A list of relative volume adjustment (2). Remarks This frame allows the user to say how much he wants to increase/decrease the volume on each channel when the file is played. The purpose is to be able to align all files to a reference volume, so that you don't have to change the volume constantly. This frame may also be used to balance adjust the audio. The volume adjustment is encoded as a fixed point decibel value, 16 bit signed integer representing (adjustment*512), giving +/- 64 dB with a precision of 0.001953125 dB. E.g. +2 dB is stored as 0x04 0x00 and -2 dB is 0xFC 0x00. There may be more than one Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Identification. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ReleaseT.title: Property ReleaseTime | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ReleaseT.summary: Property ReleaseTime Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ReleaseTime Gets or sets the release time. public Id3v2TextFrame? ReleaseTime { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The release time. Remarks The 'Release time' frame contains a timestamp describing when the audio was first released. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.RemoveFr.title: Method RemoveFrame | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.RemoveFr.summary: Method RemoveFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RemoveFrame(Id3v2Frame?) Removes the frame. public void RemoveFrame(Id3v2Frame? frame) Parameters frame Id3v2Frame The frame.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.RemoveFr.title: Method RemoveFrames | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.RemoveFr.summary: Method RemoveFrames Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll RemoveFrames<T>() Removes all frames of type T. public void RemoveFrames<T>() where T : Id3v2Frame Type Parameters T A class of type Id3v2Frame. RemoveFrames(IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame>) Removes the frames. public void RemoveFrames(IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame> frames) Parameters frames IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame> The frames. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if frames is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Reverb.h.title: Property Reverb | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Reverb.h.summary: Property Reverb Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Reverb Gets or sets the reverb. public Id3v2ReverbFrame? Reverb { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2ReverbFrame The reverb. Remarks This frame is used to adjust echoes of different kinds. There may only be one Id3v2ReverbFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Seek.htm.title: Property Seek | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Seek.htm.summary: Property Seek Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Seek Gets or sets the seek frame. public Id3v2SeekFrame? Seek { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2SeekFrame The seek value. Remarks This frame indicates where other tags in a file/stream can be found. There may only be one Id3v2SeekFrame in an Id3v2Tag. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetFrame.title: Method SetFrame | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetFrame.summary: Method SetFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SetFrame(Id3v2Frame) Updates the first matching frame if found; else, adds a new frame. public void SetFrame(Id3v2Frame frame) Parameters frame Id3v2Frame Frame to add to the Id3v2Tag. Remarks The frame needs to have the same version set as the Id3v2Tag, otherwise an InvalidVersionException will be thrown. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if frame is null. InvalidVersionException Thrown if the version of the tag does not match the version of the frame.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetFrame.title: Method SetFrames | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetFrame.summary: Method SetFrames Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SetFrames(IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame>) Updates a list of frames with a matching identifier if found; else, adds it. public void SetFrames(IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame> frames) Parameters frames IEnumerable<Id3v2Frame> The frames. Remarks The frames needs to have the same version set as the Id3v2Tag, otherwise an InvalidVersionException will be thrown. Exceptions ArgumentNullException Thrown if frames is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetSubti.title: Property SetSubtitle | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SetSubti.summary: Property SetSubtitle Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SetSubtitle Gets or sets the set subtitle. public Id3v2TextFrame? SetSubtitle { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The set subtitle. Remarks The 'Set subtitle' frame is intended for the subtitle of the part of a set this track belongs to. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Signatur.title: Property Signatures | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Signatur.summary: Property Signatures Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Signatures Gets or sets a list of signatures. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2SignatureFrame> Signatures { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2SignatureFrame> A list of signatures. Remarks This frame enables a group of frames, grouped with the 'Group identification registration', to be signed. Although signatures can reside inside the registration frame, it might be desired to store the signature elsewhere, e.g. in watermarks. There may be more than one Id3v2SignatureFrame in an Id3v2Tag, but no two may be identical. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SyncedTe.title: Property SyncedTempoCodes | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.SyncedTe.summary: Property SyncedTempoCodes Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SyncedTempoCodes Gets or sets the synced tempo codes. public Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame? SyncedTempoCodes { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame The synced tempo codes. Remarks For a more accurate description of the tempo of a musical piece this frame might be used. Each tempo code consists of one tempo part and one time part. There may only be one Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Synchron.title: Property SynchronizedLyrics | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Synchron.summary: Property SynchronizedLyrics Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll SynchronizedLyrics Gets or sets a list of synchronized lyrics. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame> SynchronizedLyrics { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame> The synchronized lyrics. Remarks This is another way of incorporating the words, said or sung lyrics, in the audio file as text, this time, however, in sync with the audio. It might also be used to describing events e.g. occurring on a stage or on the screen in sync with the audio. There may be more than one Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Language and ContentDescriptor.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TagIsExp.title: Property TagIsExperimental | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TagIsExp.summary: Property TagIsExperimental Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TagIsExperimental Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Id3v2Tag is in experimental stage. public bool TagIsExperimental { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the Id3v2Tag is in experimental stage; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later and should always be set when the Id3v2Tag is in an experimental stage.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TaggingT.title: Property TaggingTime | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TaggingT.summary: Property TaggingTime Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TaggingTime Gets or sets the tagging time. public Id3v2TextFrame? TaggingTime { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The tagging time. Remarks The 'Tagging time' frame contains a timestamp describing then the audio was tagged. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TermsOfU.title: Property TermsOfUse | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TermsOfU.summary: Property TermsOfUse Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TermsOfUse Gets or sets the terms of use. public Id3v2TermsOfUseFrame? TermsOfUse { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TermsOfUseFrame The terms of use. Remarks This frame contains a brief description of the terms of use and ownership of the file. More detailed information concerning the legal terms might be available through CopyrightInformation. There may only be one Id3v2TermsOfUseFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag. This frame has been added as of Id3v230.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TextLang.title: Property TextLanguages | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TextLang.summary: Property TextLanguages Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TextLanguages Gets or sets the text language. public Id3v2TextFrame? TextLanguages { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The text language. Remarks The 'Language(s)' frame should contain the languages of the text or lyrics in the audio file. The language is represented with three characters according to ISO-639-2. If more than one language is used in the text their language codes should follow according to their usage. Exceptions InvalidDataException thrown when the language is not a ISO-639-2 value or the language is not XXX for version Id3v2.4.0

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TextWrit.title: Property TextWriter | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TextWrit.summary: Property TextWriter Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TextWriter Gets or sets the text writer. public Id3v2TextFrame? TextWriter { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The text writer. Remarks The 'Lyricist(s)/text writer(s)' frame is intended for the writer(s) of the text or lyrics in the recording.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TimeReco.title: Property TimeRecording | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TimeReco.summary: Property TimeRecording Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TimeRecording Gets or sets the time for the recording. public Id3v2TextFrame? TimeRecording { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The time for the recording. Remarks The 'Time' frame is a numeric string in the HHMM format containing the time for the recording. This field is always four characters long. This frame has been replaced by the TDRC frame, 'Recording time' as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TitleSor.title: Property TitleSortOrder | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TitleSor.summary: Property TitleSortOrder Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TitleSortOrder Gets or sets the title sort order. public Id3v2TextFrame? TitleSortOrder { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The title sort order. Remarks The 'Title sort order' frame defines a string which should be used instead of the title (TIT2) for sorting purposes. This frame has been added as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ToString.title: Method ToString | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.ToString.summary: Method ToString Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll ToString() Returns a string that represents the current object. public override string ToString() Returns string A string that represents the current object.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackNum.title: Property TrackNumber | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackNum.summary: Property TrackNumber Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackNumber Gets or sets the track number. public Id3v2TextFrame? TrackNumber { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The track number. Remarks The 'Track number/Position in set' frame is a numeric string containing the order number of the audio-file on its original recording. This may be extended with a "/" character and a numeric string containing the total number of tracks/elements on the original recording. E.g. "4/9".

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackTit.title: Property TrackTitle | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackTit.summary: Property TrackTitle Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackTitle Gets or sets the track title. public Id3v2TextFrame? TrackTitle { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The track title.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackTit.title: Property TrackTitleDescription | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.TrackTit.summary: Property TrackTitleDescription Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TrackTitleDescription Gets or sets the track title description. public Id3v2TextFrame? TrackTitleDescription { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The track title description. Remarks The 'Subtitle/Description refinement' frame is used for information directly related to the contents title (e.g. "Op. 16" or "Performed live at wembley").

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UniqueFi.title: Property UniqueFileIdentifiers | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UniqueFi.summary: Property UniqueFileIdentifiers Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UniqueFileIdentifiers Gets or sets a list of unique file identifiers. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame> UniqueFileIdentifiers { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame> The unique file identifiers. Remarks The purpose of the Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame frame is to be able to identify the audio file in a database that may contain more information relevant to the content. All Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrames contain an URL of an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific database implementation. Questions regarding the database should be sent to the indicated email address. The URL should not be used for the actual database queries. The string "http://www.id3.org/dummy/ufid.html" should be used for tests. There may be more than one Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame frame in an Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same OwnerIdentifier.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Unsychro.title: Property UnsychronizedLyrics | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Unsychro.summary: Property UnsychronizedLyrics Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UnsychronizedLyrics Gets or sets a list of unsynchronized lyrics. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UnsynchronizedLyricsFrame> UnsychronizedLyrics { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UnsynchronizedLyricsFrame> The unsynchronized lyrics. Remarks This frame contains the lyrics of the song or a text transcription of other vocal activities. There may be more than one Id3v2UnsynchronizedLyricsFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Language and ContentDescriptor.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseCompr.title: Property UseCompression | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseCompr.summary: Property UseCompression Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseCompression Gets or sets a value indicating whether compression should be used. public bool UseCompression { get; set; } Property Value bool true if compression should be used; otherwise, false. Remarks Compression is only supported in version Id3v221 and earlier. Since no compression scheme has been decided for version Id3v221, the ID3 decoder (for now) should just ignore the entire tag if the compression property is set.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseExten.title: Property UseExtendedHeader | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseExten.summary: Property UseExtendedHeader Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseExtendedHeader Gets a value indicating whether an extended header should be added to the Id3v2Tag. public bool UseExtendedHeader { get; set; } Property Value bool true if an extended header should be added to the Id3v2Tag; otherwise, false. Remarks This property only applies for version Id3v230 and later.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseFoote.title: Property UseFooter | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseFoote.summary: Property UseFooter Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseFooter Gets or sets a value indicating whether a footer should be added to the Id3v2Tag. public bool UseFooter { get; set; } Property Value bool true if a footer should be added to the Id3v2Tag; otherwise, false. Remarks This only applies for version Id3v240 and later.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseHeade.title: Property UseHeader | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseHeade.summary: Property UseHeader Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseHeader Gets or sets a value indicating whether a header should be added to the Id3v2Tag. public bool UseHeader { get; set; } Property Value bool true if a header should be added to the Id3v2Tag; otherwise, false. Remarks For versions below Id3v240, this will always be true and can not be changed. If this is set to false, UseFooter will be set to true.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseUnsyn.title: Property UseUnsynchronization | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UseUnsyn.summary: Property UseUnsynchronization Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UseUnsynchronization Gets or sets a value indicating whether the Id3v2Tag uses unsynchronization. public bool UseUnsynchronization { get; set; } Property Value bool true if the Id3v2Tag has been unsynchronized; otherwise, false. Remarks If true the frame data will be unsynchronized as necessary.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UserDefi.title: Property UserDefinedTextInformations | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UserDefi.summary: Property UserDefinedTextInformations Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UserDefinedTextInformations Gets or sets a list of user defined text information. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UserDefinedTextInformationFrame> UserDefinedTextInformations { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UserDefinedTextInformationFrame> The user defined text information's. Remarks These frames are intended for one-string text information concerning the audio file in a similar way to the other Id3v2TextFrames. There may be more than one Id3v2UserDefinedTextInformationFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Description.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UserDefi.title: Property UserDefinedUrlLinks | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.UserDefi.summary: Property UserDefinedUrlLinks Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll UserDefinedUrlLinks Gets or sets a list of user defined URL links. public Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UserDefinedUrlLinkFrame> UserDefinedUrlLinks { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2FrameCollection<Id3v2UserDefinedUrlLinkFrame> The user defined URL links. Remarks These frames are intended for URL [URL] links concerning the audio file in a similar way to the other Id3v2UrlLinkFrame frames. There may be more than one Id3v2UserDefinedUrlLinkFrame frame in each Id3v2Tag, but only one with the same Description.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Version..title: Property Version | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.Version..summary: Property Version Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Version Gets or sets the version of the Id3v2Tag. public Id3v2Version Version { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2Version The version. Remarks When changing the version, the version of all Frames will be changed as well. Frames which don't support the new version will be removed. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when value is a valid out of valid ranges.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.WriteTo..title: Method WriteTo | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.WriteTo..summary: Method WriteTo Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll WriteTo(Stream) Writes the serialised Id3v2Tag to the supplied stream. public void WriteTo(Stream destination) Parameters destination Stream The stream to write the tag bytes into. Remarks Frames which have TagAlterPreservation or FileAlterPreservation set to false won't be written. Exceptions ArgumentNullException If destination is null.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.YearReco.title: Property YearRecording | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.YearReco.summary: Property YearRecording Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll YearRecording Gets or sets the year of the recording. public Id3v2TextFrame? YearRecording { get; set; } Property Value Id3v2TextFrame The year of the recording. Remarks The 'Year' frame is a numeric string with a year of the recording. This frames is always four characters long (until the year 10000). This frame has been replaced by the TDRC frame, 'Recording time' as of Id3v240.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2Tag.html.summary: Class Id3v2Tag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Class to store an Id3v2 tag. public sealed class Id3v2Tag : IAudioTag, IEquatable<IAudioTag> Inheritance object Id3v2Tag Implements IAudioTag IEquatable<IAudioTag> Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Extension Methods IAudioTagExtensions.GetSerializedSize(IAudioTag) IAudioTagExtensions.ToByteArray(IAudioTag) IAudioTagExtensions.TryWriteTo(IAudioTag, Span<byte>, out int) IAudioTagExtensions.WriteTo(IAudioTag, IBufferWriter<byte>) Remarks an Id3v2Tag contains a header, Id3v2Frames and optional PaddingSize. Max128Characters = 2 No string is longer than 128 characters. Max30Characters = 3 No string is longer than 30 characters. NoRestrictions = 0 No restrictions. Remarks Note that nothing is said about how many bytes is used to represent those characters, since it is encoding dependent. If a text frame consists of more than one string, the sum of the strings is restricted as stated.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2TimeStampFor.title: Enum Id3v2TimeStampFormat | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2TimeStampFor.summary: Enum Id3v2TimeStampFormat Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll The time stamp format of event timing codes. public enum Id3v2TimeStampFormat Fields AbsoluteTimeMilliseconds = 2 Absolute time, 32 bit sized, using milliseconds as unit. AbsoluteTimeMpegFrames = 1 Absolute time, 32 bit sized, using MPEG [MPEG] frames as unit. Remarks Absolute time means that every stamp contains the time from the beginning of the file.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2UniqueFileId.title: Property OwnerIdentifier | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2UniqueFileId.summary: Property OwnerIdentifier Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll OwnerIdentifier Gets or sets a null-terminated string with a URL containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found. Questions regarding the database should be sent to the indicated email address. The URL should not be used for the actual database queries. The string "http://www.id3.org/dummy/ufid.html" should be used for tests. public string OwnerIdentifier { get; set; } Property Value string Remarks For Id3v230 and later: The owner identifier must be non-empty (more than just a termination). The URL must be a valid RFC 1738 URL, use IsValidUrl(string) to check if a value is a valid URL.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Id3v2UniqueFileId.summary: Class Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Class for storing information for identifying the audio file in a database that may contain more information relevant to the content. Since standardization of such a database is beyond this document, this class only holds contact information for such a database. public sealed class Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame : Id3v2Frame, IAudioTagFrame, IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame>, IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inheritance object Id3v2Frame Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame Implements IAudioTagFrame IEquatable<IAudioTagFrame> IEquatable<Id3v2Frame> Inherited Members Id3v2Frame.MaxAllowedSize Id3v2Frame.EncryptedData Id3v2Frame.CompressedData Id3v2Frame.Cryptor Id3v2Frame.Compressor Id3v2Frame.Equals(object) Id3v2Frame.IsValidEncodingType(Id3v2FrameEncodingType) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifier<T>(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.Version Id3v2Frame.EncryptionType Id3v2Frame.GroupIdentifier Id3v2Frame.TagAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.FileAlterPreservation Id3v2Frame.IsReadOnly Id3v2Frame.UseCompression Id3v2Frame.UseEncryption Id3v2Frame.UseGroupingIdentity Id3v2Frame.UseUnsynchronization Id3v2Frame.UseDataLengthIndicator Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifier(Id3v2Version, string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidLanguageCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCountryCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidCurrencyCode(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidUrl(string) Id3v2Frame.IsValidDefaultTextString(string, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTextString(string, Id3v2FrameEncodingType, bool) Id3v2Frame.IsValidTimeStampFormat(Id3v2TimeStampFormat) Id3v2Frame.IsValidIdentifierByte(short) Id3v2Frame.IsValidVersion(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.GetIdentifierFieldLength(Id3v2Version) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) Id3v2Frame.ReadFromStream<T>(Id3v2Version, Stream, long) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks The purpose of the Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame frame is to be able to identify the audio file in a database that may contain more information relevant to the content. All Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrames contain an URL of an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found, that belongs to the organization responsible for this specific database implementation. Questions regarding the database should be sent to the indicated email address. This frame supports Id3v2Version up to and including Id3v240. Constructors Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame() Initializes a new instance of the Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame class with version Id3v230. OwnerIdentifier Gets or sets a null-terminated string with a URL containing an email address, or a link to a location where an email address can be found. Questions regarding the database should be sent to the indicated email address. The URL should not be used for the actual database queries. The string "http://www.id3.org/dummy/ufid.html" should be used for tests. Methods Equals(Id3v2Frame?) Equals the specified Id3v2Frame. Equals(Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame?) Equals the specified Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame. IsVersionSupported(Id3v2Version) Determines whether the specified version is supported by the frame.

Lyrics { get; set; } Property Value string The lyrics. Remarks The keywords "LYRICSBEGIN" and "LYRICSEND" may not be present in the lyrics. A byte in the lyrics must not have the binary value 255. The maximum length of the lyrics is 5100 bytes. Newlines are made with CR+LF sequence. If encoding the value in the specified Encoding exceeds MaxLyricsSize bytes, the value will be cut to the maximum character count which fits within MaxLyricsSize bytes.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.title: Property Description | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.summary: Property Description Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Description Gets or sets the description. public string? Description { get; set; } Property Value string The description. Remarks Description and is optional. The description can be up to 250 chars long, and will be truncated when longer.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.title: Property Filename | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.summary: Property Filename Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Filename Gets or sets the filename. public string? Filename { get; set; } Property Value string The filename. Remarks Filename can be in one of these formats: Filename only - when the image is located in the same path as the MP3 file (preferred, since if you move the mp3 file this will still be correct) Relative Path + Filename - when the image is located in a subdirectory below the MP3 file (i.e. images\cover.jpg) Full path + Filename - when the image is located in a totally different path or drive. This will not work if the image is moved or drive letters has changed, and so should be avoided if possible (i.e. c:\images\artist.jpg)

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.title: Property TimeStamps | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.Lyrics3v2ImageFil.summary: Property TimeStamps Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll TimeStamps Gets or sets a list of time stamps for the image file. public IList<TimeSpan> TimeStamps { get; } Property Value IList<TimeSpan> A list of time stamps for the image file. Remarks Timestamps are optional. If an image has a timestamp, then the visible image should automatically switch to that image on the timestamp play time, just the same as the selected lyrics line is switched based on timestamps.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MatroskaTag.html.title: Class MatroskaTag | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MatroskaTag.html.summary: Class MatroskaTag Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll Aggregates every top-level Tag entry seen inside a Matroska / WebM segment's Tags element. public sealed class MatroskaTag Inheritance object MatroskaTag Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Provides serialisation back to the Matroska Tags element bytes for round-trip writers, plus a small set of strongly-typed accessors that look up well-known SimpleTag names by EBML metadata convention. Fields AlbumLevel The Matroska album-level TargetTypeValue. TrackLevel The Matroska track-level TargetTypeValue. Properties Album Gets the album name (album-level only). Artist Gets the artist (album-level preferred, track-level fallback). Comment Gets the comment (album-level preferred, track-level fallback). Date Gets the recording date (album-level preferred, track-level fallback). Entries Gets the parsed Tag entries, one per top-level Tag element in the segment. Genre Gets the genre (album-level preferred, track-level fallback). Title Gets the album title, falling back to the track title if no album-level title exists. TrackNumber Gets the track number (track-level only by convention). Methods FromPayload(byte[]) Parses a Tags element's payload bytes (the bytes after its id+size header) into a MatroskaTag. SerializePayload() Serialises just the payload bytes of the Tags element (i.e. the concatenation of every Tag entry). ToByteArray() Serialises this tag as the on-disk bytes of a Matroska Tags element (id + size VINT + payload).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MusicMatchImage.F.title: Property FileExtension | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MusicMatchImage.F.summary: Property FileExtension Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll FileExtension Gets or sets the file extension. public string FileExtension { get; set; } Property Value string The file extension. Remarks The extension of the image when saved as a file (for example, "jpg" or "bmp"). This section is 4 bytes in length, and the data is padded with spaces (0x20) if the extension doesn't use all 4 bytes (in practice, 3-byte extensions are the most prevalent). Likewise, a MusicMatchTag without images have all spaces for this section (4 * 0x20).

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MusicMatchTag.Aud.title: Property AudioMetaDataSizes | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.MusicMatchTag.Aud.summary: Property AudioMetaDataSizes Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Assembly AudioVideoLib.dll AudioMetaDataSizes The audio meta data sizes public static int[] AudioMetaDataSizes { get; } Property Value int[] Remarks In all versions of the MusicMatch format up to and including 3.00, this audio meta data length is always 7868 bytes in length. All subsequent versions allowed three possible length for this section: 7936, 8004, and 8132 bytes. The conditions under which a particular length from these three possibilities was used is unknown. In all cases, this section is padded with dashes ($2D) to achieve this constant size.

api/AudioVideoLib.Tags.html.summary: Namespace AudioVideoLib.Tags Classes ApeBinaryItem Class used to store an ApeBinaryItem item. ApeItem Class used to store an ApeTag item. ApeItemParseEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. ApeItemParsedEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. ApeLocatorItem Class used to store an ApeLocatorItem item. ApeTag Class to store an APE tag. ApeTagReader Class to store an APE tag. ApeUtf8Item Class used to store an ApeUtf8Item item. AsfMetadataItem A single Metadata Object (MO) or Metadata Library Object (MLO) item. AsfMetadataTag Strongly-typed model of the metadata that can appear inside an ASF Header Object: the fixed-schema Content Description Object (CDO), the Extended Content Description Object (ECDO) of typed key/value pairs, and the Metadata Object (MO) / Metadata Library Object (MLO) collections. AsfTypedValue A typed value carried by an ASF Extended Content Description, Metadata, or Metadata Library item. Acts as a tagged union over the six ASF data types. AudioTagOffset Defines the offset for an IAudioTag in a System.IO.Stream AudioTagParseErrorEventArgs Event data raised when AudioTags encounters a tag reader that throws while parsing. The failing tag is skipped and the next reader is tried — the error is surfaced via this event so callers can decide whether to log it, display it, or fail. AudioTagParseEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. AudioTagParsedEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. AudioTags Represents a collection of audio tag offsets. IAudioTagExtensions Buffer-shaped convenience helpers around WriteTo(Stream). Id3v1Tag Per-field text storage for an ID3v1 tag. The source of truth is the raw byte sequence captured at parse time; a per-field Encoding override controls how those bytes are decoded and (on save) how new string values are re-encoded. This lets callers display a tag written with one encoding (e.g. UTF-8) even when the tag-level default is something else (e.g. Latin-1). Id3v1TagReader Class to store an Id3v1 tag. Id3v2AdjustmentPoint Adjustment point information for each adjustment point in an Id3v2Equalisation2Frame frame. Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame Class for storing an attached picture frame. Id3v2AudioEncryptionFrame Class for storing audio encryption. Id3v2AudioSeekPointIndexFrame Class for storing the audio seek point index. Id3v2ChannelInformation Channel information in an Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame frame. Id3v2CommentFrame Class for storing a comment. Id3v2CommercialFrame Class for storing an commercial frame. Id3v2CompressedDataMetaFrame Class for storing compressed data meta. This frame is specific for Id3v221. Id3v2EncryptedMetaFrame Class for storing an Id3v2 encrypted meta frame. Id3v2EncryptionMethodRegistrationFrame Class for storing an Id3v2 encryption method registration. Id3v2Equalisation2Frame Class for storing the equalization (2). Id3v2EqualisationBand Class to store information about an equalization band. Id3v2EqualisationFrame Class for storing the equalisation. Id3v2EventTimingCodesFrame Class for storing event timing codes. Id3v2ExperimentalRelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame Class for storing a relative volume adjustment (2). Id3v2ExtendedHeader The extended header contains information that can provide further insight in the structure of the Id3v2Tag, but is not vital to the correct parsing of the Id3v2Tag information; hence the extended header is optional. Id3v2Frame Class used to store an Id3v2Tag frame. Id3v2Frame.Id3v2FrameEncoding Static class with functions related to frame encoding for Id3v2Frames. Id3v2FrameCollection<T> Collection for frames. Id3v2FrameParseErrorEventArgs Event data raised when Id3v2TagReader encounters a frame that cannot be parsed. Id3v2FrameParseEventArgs Class for storing event data passed as argument to subscribed event handlers. Id3v2InvolvedPeopleListFrame Class for storing a list of involved people. Id3v2KeyEvent Class for storing a key event. Id3v2LinkedInformationFrame Class for storing linked information. Id3v2PositionSynchronizationFrame Class for storing the position synchronization. Id3v2PrivateFrame Class for storing a private frame. Id3v2RecommendedBufferSizeFrame Class for storing the Id3v2 recommended buffer size. Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame Class for storing a relative volume adjustment (2). Id3v2SignatureFrame Class for storing a signature. Id3v2SyncedTempoCodesFrame Class for storing synced tempo codes. Id3v2SynchronizedLyricsFrame Class for storing synchronized lyrics. Id3v2Tag Class to store an Id3v2 tag. Id3v2TagReader Class to store an Id3v2 tag. Id3v2TagRestrictions For some applications it might be desired to restrict a tag in more ways than imposed by the Id3v2 specification. Id3v2UniqueFileIdentifierFrame Class for storing information for identifying the audio file in a database that may contain more information relevant to the content. Mp4FreeFormKey Identifies a free-form (----) iTunes metadata item by its mean namespace and name key. Interfaces IAudioTag Interface which tag implementations must implement. IAudioTagFrame Interface for implementing an tag frame. IAudioTagOffset Defines the offset for an IAudioTag in a System.IO.Stream IAudioTagReader Interface for audio tag readers. IId3v2FrameCompressor Interface for handling Id3v2Frame compression/decompression. IId3v2FrameCryptor Interface for handling Id3v2Frame encryption/decryption. Enums ApeItemKey A list of pre-defined known ApeItem keys in an ApeTag. ApeItemType Defines the type of an ApeItem. ApeVersion Enumerator to indicate the APE tag version. AsfDataType Discriminator for the value types carried by an ASF Extended Content Description / Metadata / Metadata Library item. AudioTagParseErrorKind Categorises a tag-parse failure so callers can dispatch on a stable enum value instead of inspecting the raw Exception message. Id3v1Genre Enumerator values for the Genre field in an Id3v1Tag. Id3v1TrackSpeed Enumerator values for the TrackSpeed field in an extended Id3v1Tag. Id3v1Version Enumerator to indicate the Id3v1Tag version. Id3v2AttachedPictureType Picture types an Id3v2AttachedPictureFrame can have. Id3v2AudioDeliveryType Describes how audio is delivered when bought. Id3v2ChannelType Type of channels in an Id3v2RelativeVolumeAdjustment2Frame frame. Id3v2CompressionMethod Id3v2 compression method. Id3v2ContentType Possible values the content type can be. Id3v2FrameEncodingType Id3v2 encoding values used to specify how to encode text frames. Id3v2ImageEncodingRestriction Id3v240 Id3v2Tag image encoding restrictions. Id3v2ImageSizeRestriction Id3v240 Id3v2Tag image size restrictions. Id3v2InterpolationMethod The 'interpolation method' describes which method is preferred when an interpolation between the adjustment point that follows. Id3v2KeyEventType Type of Id3v2KeyEvent. Id3v2NameCode Name code in an Id3v2ReplayGainAdjustmentFrame. Id3v2OriginatorCode Originator code in an Id3v2ReplayGainAdjustmentFrame. Id3v2ReplayGainSign Sign indicator in an Id3v2ReplayGainAdjustmentFrame. Id3v2TagSizeRestriction Id3v240 Id3v2Tag size restrictions. Id3v2TextEncodingRestriction Id3v240 Id3v2Tag encoding restrictions. Id3v2TextFieldsSizeRestriction Id3v240 Id3v2Tag text fields size restrictions. Id3v2TextFrameIdentifier A list of pre-defined known Id3v2TextFrame identifiers in an Id3v2Tag. Id3v2TimeStampFormat The time stamp format of event timing codes. Id3v2UrlLinkFrameIdentifier A list of pre-defined known Id3v2UrlLinkFrame identifiers in an Id3v2Tag. Id3v2Version Enumerator to indicate the Id3v2 version. Lyrics3v2TextFieldIdentifier A list of pre-defined known Lyrics3v2TextField identifiers in a Lyrics3v2Tag. Mp4CoverArtFormat Identifies the image format carried by an MP4 covr entry. TagOrigin Provides the fields that represent the reference points of an IAudioTag in a stream.

api/AudioVideoLib.html.title: Namespace AudioVideoLib | AudioVideoLib
api/AudioVideoLib.html.summary: Namespace AudioVideoLib Classes AudioInfo Class for storing audio related info. InvalidVersionException The exception that is thrown when an invalid version is passed to a class constructor or function parameter.

api/index.html.title: API reference | AudioVideoLib
api/index.html.summary: API reference This section is generated from the XML doc comments in the source. Top-level namespaces: AudioVideoLib — shared exception types (InvalidVersionException). AudioVideoLib.Collections — NotifyingList<T> and its event args, used by tag readers to raise per-item events. AudioVideoLib.Cryptography — Crc8, Crc16 helpers used by MPEG frame checksums and ID3v2 extended-header CRC. AudioVideoLib.Formats — plain records for non-tag data: MPEG audio frames, FLAC metadata blocks, VBR headers (Xing / LAME / VBRI), RIFF / AIFF chunks, OGG pages, MP4 boxes, DSD chunks, EBML elements, BWF bext, iXML, RiffInfoTag, AiffTextChunks. AudioVideoLib.IO — stream walkers (IMediaContainer implementations), the MediaContainers registry, and the seekable buffer primitives StreamBuffer / BitStream. AudioVideoLib.Tags — tag readers and tag models (ID3v1, ID3v2, APE, Lyrics3, MusicMatch, Vorbis, MP4 / iTunes, ASF, Matroska), plus the AudioTags scanner and the shared interfaces IAudioTag, IAudioTagReader, IAudioTagOffset.

docs/container-formats.html.title: Container formats | AudioVideoLib
docs/container-formats.html.summary: Container formats Walkers in AudioVideoLib.IO. Each implements IMediaContainer and is auto-detected by MediaContainers.ReadStream(stream). Pick a walker below for its on-disk shape, the public API surface, and any quirks. Walker Container Surfaces MpaStream MPEG-1 / MPEG-2 / MPEG-2.5 audio (Layer I / II / III) frame-by-frame MpaFrame + Xing / LAME / VBRI VBR header FlacStream FLAC metadata blocks (STREAMINFO, VORBIS_COMMENT, PICTURE, …) + frames RiffStream RIFF / WAV / RIFX fmt / data + LIST INFO / id3 / bext / iXML side-channels AiffStream AIFF / AIFF-C COMM / SSND + NAME / AUTH / ANNO / COMT text chunks OggStream OGG page-by-page; codec (vorbis / opus), channels, sample rate Mp4Stream MP4 / M4A top-level boxes + moov.udta.meta.ilst iTunes metadata AsfStream ASF / WMA / WMV Header Object children + aggregated metadata tag MatroskaStream Matroska / WebM EBML header + first Segment (Info, Tags) DsfStream / DffStream DSD audio (Sony DSF, Philips DFF) DSD / fmt / data chunks + embedded ID3v2 Container-embedded metadata (MP4 ilst, ASF, Matroska tags, WAV side channels, AIFF text, DSF/DFF embedded ID3v2) is documented under Tag formats. The walkers above describe how the container is parsed and which properties carry the metadata models.

docs/container-formats/aiffstream.html.title: AiffStream — AIFF / AIFF-C | AudioVideoLib
docs/container-formats/aiffstream.html.summary: AiffStream — AIFF / AIFF-C Same shape as RIFF but big-endian. Surfaces COMM (channels, sample frames, sample size, sample rate as IEEE 80-bit extended precision, optional AIFC compression 4-CC), SSND (offset and size of audio), and the text chunks NAME / AUTH / ANNO / COMT via TextChunks. On-disk layout AIFF and AIFF-C (Apple, 1988 / 1991) are built around the FORM container — a big-endian variant of the same IFF chunk structure RIFF later adopted. The file begins with the magic FORM, followed by a 32-bit big-endian size field and the form-type identifier (AIFF or AIFC), then a sequence of chunks identified by 4-character ASCII codes. The COMM (common) chunk is mandatory and carries the essential format parameters: channel count, number of sample frames, sample size in bits, and sample rate encoded as an IEEE 754 80-bit extended-precision value. AIFF-C extends COMM with a 4-character compression identifier and an optional human-readable name. The SSND (sound data) chunk holds the raw audio samples, prefixed by offset and block-align fields. Optional text chunks supply track-level metadata: NAME, AUTH, and ANNO are flat ASCII strings; COMT carries timestamped comments keyed by an optional marker id. All chunk payloads are padded to even byte length, with the size field reporting the unpadded count. AiffStream.Chunks lists every chunk in file order with start and end offsets; the parsed COMM fields surface as Channels, SampleFrames, SampleSize, SampleRate, and Compression; SSND location and extent through SsndOffset / SsndSize; and the text metadata via AiffStream.TextChunks. For the AIFF text chunks themselves see WAV / AIFF chunks.

docs/container-formats/asfstream.html.title: AsfStream — ASF / WMA / WMV | AudioVideoLib
docs/container-formats/asfstream.html.summary: AsfStream — ASF / WMA / WMV Header Object walker. Descends the Header Object's children and recurses into the Header Extension Object to pick up Metadata Object and Metadata Library Object. Surfaces Objects (all top-level objects) and MetadataTag (the aggregated AsfMetadataTag). Duration comes from the File Properties Object's Play Duration (100-ns units). On-disk layout ASF (Microsoft Advanced Systems Format) is built around GUID-prefixed objects. Each object begins with a 16-byte GUID identifier followed by an 8-byte little-endian size field that includes the header itself, giving a minimum legal object size of 24 bytes. All multi-byte integers are little-endian; GUIDs use ASF's mixed-endian on-disk layout (three little-endian groups followed by one big-endian group), which Guid.ToByteArray() happens to produce natively. A file consists of three top-level objects in order: the Header Object, the Data Object, and an optional Index Object. The Header Object holds every metadata-bearing child — a Content Description Object (CDO) with five fixed fields (Title, Author, Copyright, Description, Rating), an Extended Content Description Object (ECDO) of typed key/value pairs, and (inside the Header Extension Object) the Metadata Object (MO) and Metadata Library Object (MLO) for per-stream typed metadata. The Data Object carries the encoded media frames; the Index Object accelerates seeking.
